search_text: ArgRewrite V.2: an Annotated Argumentative Revisions Corpus ArgRewrite V.2：带注释的论证修订语料库 Analyzing how humans revise their writings is an interesting research question, not only from an educational perspective but also in terms of artificial intelligence. Better understanding of this process could facilitate many NLP applications, from intelligent tutoring systems to supportive and collaborative writing environments. Developing these applications, however, requires revision corpora, which are not widely available. In this work, we present ArgRewrite V.2, a corpus of annotated argumentative revisions, collected from two cycles of revisions to argumentative essays about self-driving cars. Annotations are provided at different levels of purpose granularity (coarse and fine) and scope (sentential and subsentential). In addition, the corpus includes the revision goal given to each writer, essay scores, annotation verification, pre- and post-study surveys collected from participants as meta-data. The variety of revision unit scope and purpose granularity levels in ArgRewrite, along with the inclusion of new types of meta-data, can make it a useful resource for research and applications that involve revision analysis. We demonstrate some potential applications of ArgRewrite V.2 in the development of automatic revision purpose predictors, as a training source and benchmark. 分析人类如何修改自己的著作是一个有趣的研究问题，不仅从教育角度来看，而且从人工智能角度来看也是如此。更好地理解这一过程可以促进许多 NLP 应用，从智能辅导系统到支持性和协作性写作环境。然而，开发这些应用程序需要修订语料库，而这些语料库并未广泛使用。在这项工作中，我们提出了 ArgRewrite V.2，这是一个带注释的论证性修订的语料库，收集自有关自动驾驶汽车的论证性文章的两个修订周期。注释以不同级别的目的粒度（粗略和精细）和范围（句子和次句子）提供。此外，语料库还包括每个作者的复习目标、论文分数、注释验证、从参与者收集的作为元数据的学习前和学习后调查。 ArgRewrite 中的各种修订单元范围和目的粒度级别，以及新类型的元数据的包含，可以使其成为涉及修订分析的研究和应用程序的有用资源。我们展示了 ArgRewrite V.2 在自动修订目的预测器开发中的一些潜在应用，作为训练源和基准。 本文介绍了ArgRewrite V.2，一个包含论证性文章修订标注的语料库，用于支持自然语言处理和教育领域的研究。 Omid Kashefi,Tazin Afrin,Meghan Dale,Christopher Olshefski,Amanda Godley,Diane Litman,Rebecca Hwa cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Kevin Qinghong Lin,Alex Jinpeng Wang,Mattia Soldan,Michael Wray,Rui Yan,Eric Zhongcong Xu,Difei Gao,Rongcheng Tu,Wenzhe Zhao,Weijie Kong,Chengfei Cai,Hongfa Wang,Dima Damen,Bernard Ghanem,Wei Liu,Mike Zheng Shou
title: Egocentric Video-Language Pretraining
title_zh: 以自我为中心的视频语言预训练
comment: Accepted by NeurIPS 2022. Double champions at Ego4D and EPIC-Kitchens, CVPR 2022 challenges. 23 pages, 13 figures, 12 tables. Code: https://github.com/showlab/EgoVLP
comments: Accepted by NeurIPS 2022. Double champions at Ego4D and EPIC-Kitchens, CVPR 2022 challenges. 23 pages, 13 figures, 12 tables. Code: https://github.com/showlab/EgoVLP
abstract: Video-Language Pretraining (VLP), which aims to learn transferable representation to advance a wide range of video-text downstream tasks, has recently received increasing attention. Best performing works rely on large-scale, 3rd-person video-text datasets, such as HowTo100M. In this work, we exploit the recently released Ego4D dataset to pioneer Egocentric VLP along three directions. (i) We create EgoClip, a 1st-person video-text pretraining dataset comprising 3.8M clip-text pairs well-chosen from Ego4D, covering a large variety of human daily activities. (ii) We propose a novel pretraining objective, dubbed EgoNCE, which adapts video-text contrastive learning to the egocentric domain by mining egocentric-aware positive and negative samples. (iii) We introduce EgoMCQ, a development benchmark that is close to EgoClip and hence can support effective validation and fast exploration of our design decisions in EgoClip and EgoNCE. Furthermore, we demonstrate strong performance on five egocentric downstream tasks across three datasets: video-text retrieval on EPIC-KITCHENS-100; action recognition on Charades-Ego; natural language query, moment query, and object state change classification on Ego4D challenge benchmarks. The dataset and code are available at https://github.com/showlab/EgoVLP.
summary: Video-Language Pretraining (VLP), which aims to learn transferable representation to advance a wide range of video-text downstream tasks, has recently received increasing attention. Best performing works rely on large-scale, 3rd-person video-text datasets, such as HowTo100M. In this work, we exploit the recently released Ego4D dataset to pioneer Egocentric VLP along three directions. (i) We create EgoClip, a 1st-person video-text pretraining dataset comprising 3.8M clip-text pairs well-chosen from Ego4D, covering a large variety of human daily activities. (ii) We propose a novel pretraining objective, dubbed EgoNCE, which adapts video-text contrastive learning to the egocentric domain by mining egocentric-aware positive and negative samples. (iii) We introduce EgoMCQ, a development benchmark that is close to EgoClip and hence can support effective validation and fast exploration of our design decisions in EgoClip and EgoNCE. Furthermore, we demonstrate strong performance on five egocentric downstream tasks across three datasets: video-text retrieval on EPIC-KITCHENS-100; action recognition on Charades-Ego; natural language query, moment query, and object state change classification on Ego4D challenge benchmarks. The dataset and code are available at https://github.com/showlab/EgoVLP.
abstract_zh: 视频语言预训练（VLP）旨在学习可转移表示以推进广泛的视频文本下游任务，最近受到越来越多的关注。表现最佳的作品依赖于大规模的第三人称视频文本数据集，例如 HowTo100M。在这项工作中，我们利用最近发布的 Ego4D 数据集，沿着三个方向开创以自我为中心的 VLP。 (i) 我们创建了 EgoClip，这是一个第一人称视频文本预训练数据集，包含从 Ego4D 中精心挑选的 380 万个剪辑文本对，涵盖了各种各样的人类日常活动。 (ii) 我们提出了一种新颖的预训练目标，称为 EgoNCE，它通过挖掘自我中心意识的正样本和负样本，将视频文本对比学习适应自我中心领域。 (iii) 我们引入了 EgoMCQ，这是一个接近 EgoClip 的开发基准，因此可以支持对 EgoClip 和 EgoNCE 中的设计决策进行有效验证和快速探索。此外，我们在三个数据集上的五个以自我为中心的下游任务上展示了强大的性能：EPIC-KITCHENS-100 上的视频文本检索； Charades-Ego 上的动作识别； Ego4D 挑战基准上的自然语言查询、矩查询和对象状态变化分类。数据集和代码可在 https://github.com/showlab/EgoVLP 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于第一视角视频-文本预训练的方法，利用Ego4D数据集构建新数据集、设计预训练目标并建立评估基准。
AI.motivation: 现有视频-语言预训练方法依赖第三方视角数据集，缺乏针对第一视角场景的系统性研究，而第一视角视频在日常活动理解中具有独特价值。
AI.method: 构建包含380万第一视角视频-文本对的EgoClip数据集，设计适应第一视角的EgoNCE对比学习目标，并创建与EgoClip匹配的EgoMCQ基准用于验证。
AI.result: 在EPIC-KITCHENS-100、Charades-Ego和Ego4D等三个数据集的五个下游任务中均取得优异性能，代码和数据集已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为第一视角视频-语言理解提供了有效预训练框架和基准，推动了相关领域的发展。

authors_text: Jiaju Qi,Lei Lei,Kan Zheng,Simon X. Yang
title: Joint Energy Dispatch and Unit Commitment in Microgrids Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 基于深度强化学习的微电网联合能源调度与机组承诺
comment: The title, authors, and experimental results of this paper have all been extensively updated. Since the title has been modified and the version is no longer updated, the new paper has been submitted on arxiv
comments: The title, authors, and experimental results of this paper have all been extensively updated. Since the title has been modified and the version is no longer updated, the new paper has been submitted on arxiv
abstract: Nowadays, the application of microgrids (MG) with renewable energy is becoming more and more extensive, which creates a strong need for dynamic energy management. In this paper, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is applied to learn an optimal policy for making joint energy dispatch (ED) and unit commitment (UC) decisions in an isolated MG, with the aim for reducing the total power generation cost on the premise of ensuring the supply-demand balance. In order to overcome the challenge of discrete-continuous hybrid action space due to joint ED and UC, we propose a DRL algorithm, i.e., the hybrid action finite-horizon DDPG (HAFH-DDPG), that seamlessly integrates two classical DRL algorithms, i.e., deep Q-network (DQN) and deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG), based on a finite-horizon dynamic programming (DP) framework. Moreover, a diesel generator (DG) selection strategy is presented to support a simplified action space for reducing the computation complexity of this algorithm. Finally, the effectiveness of our proposed algorithm is verified through comparison with several baseline algorithms by experiments with real-world data set.
summary: Nowadays, the application of microgrids (MG) with renewable energy is becoming more and more extensive, which creates a strong need for dynamic energy management. In this paper, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is applied to learn an optimal policy for making joint energy dispatch (ED) and unit commitment (UC) decisions in an isolated MG, with the aim for reducing the total power generation cost on the premise of ensuring the supply-demand balance. In order to overcome the challenge of discrete-continuous hybrid action space due to joint ED and UC, we propose a DRL algorithm, i.e., the hybrid action finite-horizon DDPG (HAFH-DDPG), that seamlessly integrates two classical DRL algorithms, i.e., deep Q-network (DQN) and deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG), based on a finite-horizon dynamic programming (DP) framework. Moreover, a diesel generator (DG) selection strategy is presented to support a simplified action space for reducing the computation complexity of this algorithm. Finally, the effectiveness of our proposed algorithm is verified through comparison with several baseline algorithms by experiments with real-world data set.
abstract_zh: 如今，可再生能源微电网（MG）的应用越来越广泛，对动态能源管理产生了强烈需求。本文应用深度强化学习（DRL）来学习孤立MG中联合能源调度（ED）和机组组合（UC）决策的最优策略，旨在在保证供需平衡的前提下降低总发电成本。为了克服联合 ED 和 UC 带来的离散连续混合动作空间的挑战，我们提出了一种 DRL 算法，即混合动作有限范围 DDPG (HAFH-DDPG)，它基于有限范围动态规划 (DP) 框架，无缝集成了两种经典的 DRL 算法，即深度 Q 网络 (DQN) 和深度确定性策略梯度 (DDPG)。此外，提出了一种柴油发电机（DG）选择策略来支持简化的动作空间，从而降低该算法的计算复杂度。最后，通过真实世界数据集的实验，通过与几种基线算法的比较，验证了我们提出的算法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于深度强化学习的算法解决微电网联合能量调度与机组组合问题，以降低发电成本并确保供需平衡。
AI.motivation: 微电网中可再生能源的广泛应用需要动态能源管理，但现有方法难以处理联合调度中的离散-连续混合动作空间问题。
AI.method: 提出HAFH-DDPG算法，融合DQN与DDPG并基于有限时间动态规划框架，结合柴油发电机选择策略简化动作空间。
AI.result: 通过真实数据集实验验证，所提算法在降低发电成本方面优于多种基线算法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了微电网联合调度难题，为复杂能源系统优化提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出基于深度强化学习的算法解决微电网联合能量调度与机组组合问题，以降低发电成本并确保供需平衡。

search_text: Joint Energy Dispatch and Unit Commitment in Microgrids Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning 基于深度强化学习的微电网联合能源调度与机组承诺 Nowadays, the application of microgrids (MG) with renewable energy is becoming more and more extensive, which creates a strong need for dynamic energy management. In this paper, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is applied to learn an optimal policy for making joint energy dispatch (ED) and unit commitment (UC) decisions in an isolated MG, with the aim for reducing the total power generation cost on the premise of ensuring the supply-demand balance. In order to overcome the challenge of discrete-continuous hybrid action space due to joint ED and UC, we propose a DRL algorithm, i.e., the hybrid action finite-horizon DDPG (HAFH-DDPG), that seamlessly integrates two classical DRL algorithms, i.e., deep Q-network (DQN) and deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG), based on a finite-horizon dynamic programming (DP) framework. Moreover, a diesel generator (DG) selection strategy is presented to support a simplified action space for reducing the computation complexity of this algorithm. Finally, the effectiveness of our proposed algorithm is verified through comparison with several baseline algorithms by experiments with real-world data set. 如今，可再生能源微电网（MG）的应用越来越广泛，对动态能源管理产生了强烈需求。本文应用深度强化学习（DRL）来学习孤立MG中联合能源调度（ED）和机组组合（UC）决策的最优策略，旨在在保证供需平衡的前提下降低总发电成本。为了克服联合 ED 和 UC 带来的离散连续混合动作空间的挑战，我们提出了一种 DRL 算法，即混合动作有限范围 DDPG (HAFH-DDPG)，它基于有限范围动态规划 (DP) 框架，无缝集成了两种经典的 DRL 算法，即深度 Q 网络 (DQN) 和深度确定性策略梯度 (DDPG)。此外，提出了一种柴油发电机（DG）选择策略来支持简化的动作空间，从而降低该算法的计算复杂度。最后，通过真实世界数据集的实验，通过与几种基线算法的比较，验证了我们提出的算法的有效性。 本文提出基于深度强化学习的算法解决微电网联合能量调度与机组组合问题，以降低发电成本并确保供需平衡。 Jiaju Qi,Lei Lei,Kan Zheng,Simon X. Yang cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CE

authors_text: Jan Zuiderveld
title: Style-Content Disentanglement in Language-Image Pretraining Representations for Zero-Shot Sketch-to-Image Synthesis
title_zh: 用于零样本草图到图像合成的语言图像预训练表示中的风格内容解开
abstract: In this work, we propose and validate a framework to leverage language-image pretraining representations for training-free zero-shot sketch-to-image synthesis. We show that disentangled content and style representations can be utilized to guide image generators to employ them as sketch-to-image generators without (re-)training any parameters. Our approach for disentangling style and content entails a simple method consisting of elementary arithmetic assuming compositionality of information in representations of input sketches. Our results demonstrate that this approach is competitive with state-of-the-art instance-level open-domain sketch-to-image models, while only depending on pretrained off-the-shelf models and a fraction of the data.
summary: In this work, we propose and validate a framework to leverage language-image pretraining representations for training-free zero-shot sketch-to-image synthesis. We show that disentangled content and style representations can be utilized to guide image generators to employ them as sketch-to-image generators without (re-)training any parameters. Our approach for disentangling style and content entails a simple method consisting of elementary arithmetic assuming compositionality of information in representations of input sketches. Our results demonstrate that this approach is competitive with state-of-the-art instance-level open-domain sketch-to-image models, while only depending on pretrained off-the-shelf models and a fraction of the data.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出并验证了一个框架，利用语言图像预训练表示来进行免训练的零样本草图到图像的合成。我们表明，解开的内容和风格表示可用于指导图像生成器将它们用作草图到图像生成器，而无需（重新）训练任何参数。我们解开风格和内容的方法需要一种简单的方法，该方法由假设输入草图表示中信息的组合性的基本算术组成。我们的结果表明，这种方法与最先进的实例级开放域草图到图像模型具有竞争力，同时仅依赖于预训练的现成模型和一小部分数据。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需训练的零样本草图到图像合成框架，通过解耦预训练语言-图像表示中的风格与内容信息实现生成。
AI.motivation: 现有草图到图像生成方法依赖大量数据和参数训练，而本文旨在利用预训练模型的表示能力，在无额外训练情况下完成零样本生成任务。
AI.method: 假设输入草图表示具有组合性，通过简单算术操作解耦风格与内容表示，直接指导图像生成器进行合成。
AI.result: 该方法在实例级开放领域草图到图像任务中表现与最先进模型相当，仅需预训练模型和少量数据支持。
AI.conclusion: 解耦预训练表示中的风格内容信息可有效用于零样本生成，证明了现成模型在跨模态任务中的潜力。

authors_text: Ali Noori
title: Identification via Retinal Vessels Combining LBP and HOG
title_zh: 结合 LBP 和 HOG 的视网膜血管识别
abstract: With development of information technology and necessity for high security, using different identification methods has become very important. Each biometric feature has its own advantages and disadvantages and choosing each of them depends on our usage. Retinal scanning is a bio scale method for identification. The retina is composed of vessels and optical disk. The vessels distribution pattern is one the remarkable retinal identification methods. In this paper, a new approach is presented for identification via retinal images using LBP and hog methods. In the proposed method, it will be tried to separate the retinal vessels accurately via machine vision techniques which will have good sustainability in rotation and size change. HOG-based or LBP-based methods or their combination can be used for separation and also HSV color space can be used too. Having extracted the features, the similarity criteria can be used for identification. The implementation of proposed method and its comparison with one of the newly-presented methods in this area shows better performance of the proposed method.
summary: With development of information technology and necessity for high security, using different identification methods has become very important. Each biometric feature has its own advantages and disadvantages and choosing each of them depends on our usage. Retinal scanning is a bio scale method for identification. The retina is composed of vessels and optical disk. The vessels distribution pattern is one the remarkable retinal identification methods. In this paper, a new approach is presented for identification via retinal images using LBP and hog methods. In the proposed method, it will be tried to separate the retinal vessels accurately via machine vision techniques which will have good sustainability in rotation and size change. HOG-based or LBP-based methods or their combination can be used for separation and also HSV color space can be used too. Having extracted the features, the similarity criteria can be used for identification. The implementation of proposed method and its comparison with one of the newly-presented methods in this area shows better performance of the proposed method.
abstract_zh: 随着信息技术的发展和高安全性的需要，使用不同的识别方法变得非常重要。每个生物识别功能都有其自身的优点和缺点，选择每个生物识别功能取决于我们的使用情况。视网膜扫描是一种生物尺度的识别方法。视网膜由血管和视盘组成。血管分布模式是显着的视网膜识别方法之一。在本文中，提出了一种使用 LBP 和 hog 方法通过视网膜图像进行识别的新方法。在所提出的方法中，将尝试通过机器视觉技术准确地分离视网膜血管，这将在旋转和尺寸变化方面具有良好的可持续性。基于HOG或基于LBP的方法或它们的组合可以用于分离，也可以使用HSV颜色空间。提取特征后，可以使用相似性标准进行识别。所提出方法的实现及其与该领域新提出的方法之一的比较表明所提出方法具有更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合LBP和HOG方法的视网膜血管识别技术，以提高生物特征识别的准确性和鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 随着信息安全需求提升，传统生物特征识别方法存在局限性，需探索更可靠的视网膜识别技术以增强安全性与适应性。
AI.method: 通过机器视觉技术分离视网膜血管，结合LBP（局部二值模式）、HOG（方向梯度直方图）及HSV颜色空间提取特征，并利用相似性准则进行身份识别。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在旋转和尺寸变化下表现稳定，相较于现有方法具有更优的识别性能。
AI.conclusion: 融合LBP与HOG的视网膜识别方法能有效提升特征提取的准确性，为高安全场景提供可行的生物识别方案。

authors_text: Lena Maier-Hein,Annika Reinke,Patrick Godau,Minu D. Tizabi,Florian Buettner,Evangelia Christodoulou,Ben Glocker,Fabian Isensee,Jens Kleesiek,Michal Kozubek,Mauricio Reyes,Michael A. title: Metrics reloaded: Recommendations for image analysis validation
title_zh: 重新加载指标：图像分析验证建议
comment: Shared first authors: Lena Maier-Hein, Annika Reinke. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2104.05642 Published in Nature Methods
comments: Shared first authors: Lena Maier-Hein, Annika Reinke. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2104.05642 Published in Nature Methods
abstract: Increasing evidence shows that flaws in machine learning (ML) algorithm validation are an underestimated global problem. Particularly in automatic biomedical image analysis, chosen performance metrics often do not reflect the domain interest, thus failing to adequately measure scientific progress and hindering translation of ML techniques into practice. To overcome this, our large international expert consortium created Metrics Reloaded, a comprehensive framework guiding researchers in the problem-aware selection of metrics. Following the convergence of ML methodology across application domains, Metrics Reloaded fosters the convergence of validation methodology. The framework was developed in a multi-stage Delphi process and is based on the novel concept of a problem fingerprint - a structured representation of the given problem that captures all aspects that are relevant for metric selection, from the domain interest to the properties of the target structure(s), data set and algorithm output. Based on the problem fingerprint, users are guided through the process of choosing and applying appropriate validation metrics while being made aware of potential pitfalls. Metrics Reloaded targets image analysis problems that can be interpreted as a classification task at image, object or pixel level, namely image-level classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, and instance segmentation tasks. To improve the user experience, we implemented the framework in the Metrics Reloaded online tool, which also provides a point of access to explore weaknesses, strengths and specific recommendations for the most common validation metrics. The broad applicability of our framework across domains is demonstrated by an instantiation for various biological and medical image analysis use cases.
summary: Increasing evidence shows that flaws in machine learning (ML) algorithm validation are an underestimated global problem. Particularly in automatic biomedical image analysis, chosen performance metrics often do not reflect the domain interest, thus failing to adequately measure scientific progress and hindering translation of ML techniques into practice. To overcome this, our large international expert consortium created Metrics Reloaded, a comprehensive framework guiding researchers in the problem-aware selection of metrics. Following the convergence of ML methodology across application domains, Metrics Reloaded fosters the convergence of validation methodology. The framework was developed in a multi-stage Delphi process and is based on the novel concept of a problem fingerprint - a structured representation of the given problem that captures all aspects that are relevant for metric selection, from the domain interest to the properties of the target structure(s), data set and algorithm output. Based on the problem fingerprint, users are guided through the process of choosing and applying appropriate validation metrics while being made aware of potential pitfalls. Metrics Reloaded targets image analysis problems that can be interpreted as a classification task at image, object or pixel level, namely image-level classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, and instance segmentation tasks. To improve the user experience, we implemented the framework in the Metrics Reloaded online tool, which also provides a point of access to explore weaknesses, strengths and specific recommendations for the most common validation metrics. The broad applicability of our framework across domains is demonstrated by an instantiation for various biological and medical image analysis use cases.
abstract_zh: 越来越多的证据表明，机器学习 (ML) 算法验证中的缺陷是一个被低估的全球性问题。特别是在自动生物医学图像分析中，所选的性能指标通常不能反映领域的兴趣，因此无法充分衡量科学进步并阻碍机器学习技术转化为实践。为了克服这个问题，我们的大型国际专家联盟创建了 Metrics Reloaded，这是一个综合框架，指导研究人员以有问题意识的方式选择指标。随着跨应用领域的机器学习方法的融合，Metrics Reloaded 促进了验证方法的融合。该框架是在多阶段 Delphi 流程中开发的，基于问题指纹的新颖概念 - 给定问题的结构化表示，捕获与度量选择相关的所有方面，从领域兴趣到目标结构、数据集和算法输出的属性。根据问题指纹，引导用户完成选择和应用适当验证指标的过程，同时了解潜在的陷阱。 Metrics Reloaded 的目标是图像分析问题，这些问题可以解释为图像、对象或像素级别的分类任务，即图像级分类、对象检测、语义分割和实例分割任务。为了改善用户体验，我们在 Metrics Reloaded 在线工具中实现了该框架，该工具还提供了一个访问点来探索最常见验证指标的弱点、优势和具体建议。我们的框架跨领域的广泛适用性通过各种生物和医学图像分析用例的实例化得到了证明。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Metrics Reloaded的框架，旨在通过问题指纹概念指导图像分析领域的验证指标选择，并开发了在线工具以提升用户使用体验。
AI.motivation: 当前机器学习算法在生物医学图像分析中的验证指标常无法反映领域需求，导致科学进展评估不足及技术转化受阻，亟需统一且问题导向的验证方法。
AI.method: 通过多阶段德尔菲法汇聚国际专家意见，构建包含问题指纹（涵盖领域需求、目标结构特性、数据集及算法输出等维度）的框架，并开发配套在线工具。
AI.result: 框架在生物医学等多领域场景中验证有效，工具可帮助用户识别常见指标的优缺点及适用场景，促进跨领域验证方法的统一。
AI.conclusion: Metrics Reloaded通过问题导向的指标选择框架，解决了图像分析验证中的领域适配性问题，为机器学习技术的临床转化提供了方法论支持。

search_text: Metrics reloaded: Recommendations for image analysis validation 重新加载指标：图像分析验证建议 Increasing evidence shows that flaws in machine learning (ML) algorithm validation are an underestimated global problem. Particularly in automatic biomedical image analysis, chosen performance metrics often do not reflect the domain interest, thus failing to adequately measure scientific progress and hindering translation of ML techniques into practice. To overcome this, our large international expert consortium created Metrics Reloaded, a comprehensive framework guiding researchers in the problem-aware selection of metrics. Following the convergence of ML methodology across application domains, Metrics Reloaded fosters the convergence of validation methodology. The framework was developed in a multi-stage Delphi process and is based on the novel concept of a problem fingerprint - a structured representation of the given problem that captures all aspects that are relevant for metric selection, from the domain interest to the properties of the target structure(s), data set and algorithm output. Based on the problem fingerprint, users are guided through the process of choosing and applying appropriate validation metrics while being made aware of potential pitfalls. Metrics Reloaded targets image analysis problems that can be interpreted as a classification task at image, object or pixel level, namely image-level classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, and instance segmentation tasks. To improve the user experience, we implemented the framework in the Metrics Reloaded online tool, which also provides a point of access to explore weaknesses, strengths and specific recommendations for the most common validation metrics. The broad applicability of our framework across domains is demonstrated by an instantiation for various biological and medical image analysis use cases. 越来越多的证据表明，机器学习 (ML) 算法验证中的缺陷是一个被低估的全球性问题。特别是在自动生物医学图像分析中，所选的性能指标通常不能反映领域的兴趣，因此无法充分衡量科学进步并阻碍机器学习技术转化为实践。为了克服这个问题，我们的大型国际专家联盟创建了 Metrics Reloaded，这是一个综合框架，指导研究人员以有问题意识的方式选择指标。随着跨应用领域的机器学习方法的融合，Metrics Reloaded 促进了验证方法的融合。该框架是在多阶段 Delphi 流程中开发的，基于问题指纹的新颖概念 - 给定问题的结构化表示，捕获与度量选择相关的所有方面，从领域兴趣到目标结构、数据集和算法输出的属性。根据问题指纹，引导用户完成选择和应用适当验证指标的过程，同时了解潜在的陷阱。 Metrics Reloaded 的目标是图像分析问题，这些问题可以解释为图像、对象或像素级别的分类任务，即图像级分类、对象检测、语义分割和实例分割任务。为了改善用户体验，我们在 Metrics Reloaded 在线工具中实现了该框架，该工具还提供了一个访问点来探索最常见验证指标的弱点、优势和具体建议。我们的框架跨领域的广泛适用性通过各种生物和医学图像分析用例的实例化得到了证明。 本文提出了一种名为Metrics Reloaded的框架，旨在通过问题指纹概念指导图像分析领域的验证指标选择，并开发了在线工具以提升用户使用体验。 Lena Maier-Hein,Annika Reinke,Patrick Godau,Minu D. authors_text: Hadrien Reynaud,Athanasios Vlontzos,Mischa Dombrowski,Ciarán Lee,Arian Beqiri,Paul Leeson,Bernhard Kainz
title: D'ARTAGNAN: Counterfactual Video Generation
title_zh: D'ARTAGNAN：反事实视频生成
abstract: Causally-enabled machine learning frameworks could help clinicians to identify the best course of treatments by answering counterfactual questions. We explore this path for the case of echocardiograms by looking into the variation of the Left Ventricle Ejection Fraction, the most essential clinical metric gained from these examinations. We combine deep neural networks, twin causal networks and generative adversarial methods for the first time to build D'ARTAGNAN (Deep ARtificial Twin-Architecture GeNerAtive Networks), a novel causal generative model. We demonstrate the soundness of our approach on a synthetic dataset before applying it to cardiac ultrasound videos to answer the question: "What would this echocardiogram look like if the patient had a different ejection fraction?". To do so, we generate new ultrasound videos, retaining the video style and anatomy of the original patient, while modifying the Ejection Fraction conditioned on a given input. We achieve an SSIM score of 0.79 and an R2 score of 0.51 on the counterfactual videos. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/HReynaud/dartagnan.
summary: Causally-enabled machine learning frameworks could help clinicians to identify the best course of treatments by answering counterfactual questions. We explore this path for the case of echocardiograms by looking into the variation of the Left Ventricle Ejection Fraction, the most essential clinical metric gained from these examinations. We combine deep neural networks, twin causal networks and generative adversarial methods for the first time to build D'ARTAGNAN (Deep ARtificial Twin-Architecture GeNerAtive Networks), a novel causal generative model. We demonstrate the soundness of our approach on a synthetic dataset before applying it to cardiac ultrasound videos to answer the question: "What would this echocardiogram look like if the patient had a different ejection fraction?". To do so, we generate new ultrasound videos, retaining the video style and anatomy of the original patient, while modifying the Ejection Fraction conditioned on a given input. We achieve an SSIM score of 0.79 and an R2 score of 0.51 on the counterfactual videos. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/HReynaud/dartagnan.
abstract_zh: 支持因果关系的机器学习框架可以帮助临床医生通过回答反事实问题来确定最佳治疗方案。我们通过研究左心室射血分数（从这些检查中获得的最重要的临床指标）的变化来探索超声心动图的这条路径。我们首次将深度神经网络、孪生因果网络和生成对抗方法结合起来，构建了 D'ARTAGNAN（深度人工孪生架构 GeNerAtive Networks），一种新颖的因果生成模型。我们在合成数据集上证明了我们的方法的可靠性，然后将其应用于心脏超声视频来回答以下问题：“如果患者有不同的射血分数，超声心动图会是什么样子？”。为此，我们生成新的超声视频，保留原始患者的视频风格和解剖结构，同时根据给定输入修改射血分数。我们在反事实视频上获得了 0.79 的 SSIM 分数和 0.51 的 R2 分数。代码和模型可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/HReynaud/dartagnan。

AI.tldr: 本文提出D'ARTAGNAN模型，通过因果生成方法生成修改左心室射血分数（LVEF）的反事实超声心动图视频，同时保留原始视频风格和解剖结构。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以有效生成具有因果解释性的医学影像反事实场景，而临床需要通过改变关键指标（如LVEF）预测不同治疗方案下的影像表现以辅助决策。
AI.method: 融合深度神经网络、双因果网络与生成对抗网络（GAN），构建首个因果生成模型D'ARTAGNAN，通过条件生成实现LVEF值修改。
AI.result: 在合成数据集验证后，模型生成的反事实超声视频达到SSIM 0.79和R² 0.51的评估指标，成功保留原始视频风格与解剖特征。
AI.conclusion: D'ARTAGNAN证明了因果生成模型在医学影像反事实生成中的可行性，为临床治疗方案预测提供了新工具，未来可扩展至更多临床指标分析。

authors_text: Benoit Dufumier,Carlo Alberto Barbano,Robin Louiset,Edouard Duchesnay,Pietro Gori
title: Integrating Prior Knowledge in Contrastive Learning with Kernel
title_zh: 将对比学习中的先验知识与内核相结合
abstract: Data augmentation is a crucial component in unsupervised contrastive learning (CL). It determines how positive samples are defined and, ultimately, the quality of the learned representation. In this work, we open the door to new perspectives for CL by integrating prior knowledge, given either by generative models -- viewed as prior representations -- or weak attributes in the positive and negative sampling. To this end, we use kernel theory to propose a novel loss, called decoupled uniformity, that i) allows the integration of prior knowledge and ii) removes the negative-positive coupling in the original InfoNCE loss. We draw a connection between contrastive learning and conditional mean embedding theory to derive tight bounds on the downstream classification loss. In an unsupervised setting, we empirically demonstrate that CL benefits from generative models to improve its representation both on natural and medical images. In a weakly supervised scenario, our framework outperforms other unconditional and conditional CL approaches.
summary: Data augmentation is a crucial component in unsupervised contrastive learning (CL). It determines how positive samples are defined and, ultimately, the quality of the learned representation. In this work, we open the door to new perspectives for CL by integrating prior knowledge, given either by generative models -- viewed as prior representations -- or weak attributes in the positive and negative sampling. To this end, we use kernel theory to propose a novel loss, called decoupled uniformity, that i) allows the integration of prior knowledge and ii) removes the negative-positive coupling in the original InfoNCE loss. We draw a connection between contrastive learning and conditional mean embedding theory to derive tight bounds on the downstream classification loss. In an unsupervised setting, we empirically demonstrate that CL benefits from generative models to improve its representation both on natural and medical images. In a weakly supervised scenario, our framework outperforms other unconditional and conditional CL approaches.
abstract_zh: 数据增强是无监督对比学习（CL）的重要组成部分。它决定了如何定义正样本，并最终决定了学习表示的质量。在这项工作中，我们通过整合先验知识，为 CL 的新视角打开了大门，先验知识由生成模型（被视为先验表示）或正负采样中的弱属性给出。为此，我们使用核理论提出了一种新颖的损失，称为解耦均匀性，i）允许整合先验知识，ii）消除原始 InfoNCE 损失中的负正耦合。我们将对比学习和条件均值嵌入理论联系起来，得出下游分类损失的严格界限。在无监督的环境中，我们凭经验证明 CL 受益于生成模型，可以改善其在自然图像和医学图像上的表示。在弱监督场景中，我们的框架优于其他无条件和条件 CL 方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于核理论的新型对比学习损失函数，通过整合先验知识提升表示学习效果。
AI.motivation: 传统对比学习依赖数据增强定义正样本，但难以有效利用生成模型等先验知识，限制了表示质量的提升。
AI.method: 提出解耦均匀性损失函数，结合核方法整合先验知识并消除正负样本耦合，建立对比学习与条件均值嵌入理论的联系以推导分类损失界。
AI.result: 在自然与医学图像的无监督任务中，生成模型辅助提升表示性能；弱监督场景下优于现有无条件/有条件对比学习方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过有效整合先验知识改进对比学习，为无监督与弱监督场景提供新思路，未来可探索更复杂的先验知识融合方式。

search_text: Integrating Prior Knowledge in Contrastive Learning with Kernel 将对比学习中的先验知识与内核相结合 Data augmentation is a crucial component in unsupervised contrastive learning (CL). It determines how positive samples are defined and, ultimately, the quality of the learned representation. In this work, we open the door to new perspectives for CL by integrating prior knowledge, given either by generative models -- viewed as prior representations -- or weak attributes in the positive and negative sampling. To this end, we use kernel theory to propose a novel loss, called decoupled uniformity, that i) allows the integration of prior knowledge and ii) removes the negative-positive coupling in the original InfoNCE loss. We draw a connection between contrastive learning and conditional mean embedding theory to derive tight bounds on the downstream classification loss. In an unsupervised setting, we empirically demonstrate that CL benefits from generative models to improve its representation both on natural and medical images. In a weakly supervised scenario, our framework outperforms other unconditional and conditional CL approaches. 数据增强是无监督对比学习（CL）的重要组成部分。它决定了如何定义正样本，并最终决定了学习表示的质量。在这项工作中，我们通过整合先验知识，为 CL 的新视角打开了大门，先验知识由生成模型（被视为先验表示）或正负采样中的弱属性给出。为此，我们使用核理论提出了一种新颖的损失，称为解耦均匀性，i）允许整合先验知识，ii）消除原始 InfoNCE 损失中的负正耦合。我们将对比学习和条件均值嵌入理论联系起来，得出下游分类损失的严格界限。在无监督的环境中，我们凭经验证明 CL 受益于生成模型，可以改善其在自然图像和医学图像上的表示。在弱监督场景中，我们的框架优于其他无条件和条件 CL 方法。 本文提出一种基于核理论的新型对比学习损失函数，通过整合先验知识提升表示学习效果。 Benoit Dufumier,Carlo Alberto Barbano,Robin Louiset,Edouard Duchesnay,Pietro Gori cs.CV

authors_text: Shreyas Bhat,Joseph B. Lyons,Cong Shi,X. Jessie Yang
title: Clustering Trust Dynamics in a Human-Robot Sequential Decision-Making Task
title_zh: 人机顺序决策任务中的信任动态聚类
abstract: In this paper, we present a framework for trust-aware sequential decision-making in a human-robot team. We model the problem as a finite-horizon Markov Decision Process with a reward-based performance metric, allowing the robotic agent to make trust-aware recommendations. Results of a human-subject experiment show that the proposed trust update model is able to accurately capture the human agent's moment-to-moment trust changes. Moreover, we cluster the participants' trust dynamics into three categories, namely, Bayesian decision makers, oscillators, and disbelievers, and identify personal characteristics that could be used to predict which type of trust dynamics a person will belong to. We find that the disbelievers are less extroverted, less agreeable, and have lower expectations toward the robotic agent, compared to the Bayesian decision makers and oscillators. The oscillators are significantly more frustrated than the Bayesian decision makers.
summary: In this paper, we present a framework for trust-aware sequential decision-making in a human-robot team. We model the problem as a finite-horizon Markov Decision Process with a reward-based performance metric, allowing the robotic agent to make trust-aware recommendations. Results of a human-subject experiment show that the proposed trust update model is able to accurately capture the human agent's moment-to-moment trust changes. Moreover, we cluster the participants' trust dynamics into three categories, namely, Bayesian decision makers, oscillators, and disbelievers, and identify personal characteristics that could be used to predict which type of trust dynamics a person will belong to. We find that the disbelievers are less extroverted, less agreeable, and have lower expectations toward the robotic agent, compared to the Bayesian decision makers and oscillators. The oscillators are significantly more frustrated than the Bayesian decision makers.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一个在人机团队中进行信任感知顺序决策的框架。我们将问题建模为具有基于奖励的性能指标的有限范围马尔可夫决策过程，允许机器人代理提出信任感知的建议。人体实验结果表明，所提出的信任更新模型能够准确捕获人类代理的即时信任变化。此外，我们将参与者的信任动态分为三类，即贝叶斯决策者、震荡者和怀疑者，并确定可用于预测一个人属于哪种类型的信任动态的个人特征。我们发现，与贝叶斯决策者和震荡者相比，怀疑者不太外向，不太友善，对机器人代理的期望也较低。振荡者比贝叶斯决策者更加沮丧。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于人类-机器人团队信任感知序贯决策的框架，并通过聚类分析人类信任动态的三种类型。
AI.motivation: 为提升人类与机器人协作效率，需理解并建模人类在序贯决策中的信任动态变化及其影响因素。
AI.method: 将问题建模为有限时间地平线马尔可夫决策过程（MDP），结合奖励性能指标，通过人类受试者实验验证信任更新模型，并利用聚类方法划分信任动态类型。
AI.result: 信任更新模型准确捕捉了人类实时信任变化，参与者被分为贝叶斯决策者、振荡者和怀疑者三类，其中怀疑者外向性、宜人性更低且对机器人期望更低，振荡者比贝叶斯决策者更易产生挫败感。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效建模了信任动态，个人特质可预测信任行为类型，为个性化人机协作设计提供依据。

authors_text: M. C. Diamantini,C. A. Trugenberger
title: Mirror modular cloning and fast quantum associative retrieval
title_zh: 镜像模块化克隆和快速量子关联检索
abstract: We show that a quantum state can be perfectly cloned up to global mirroring with a unitary transformation that depends on one single parameter. We then show that this is equivalent to "perfect" cloning for quantum associative memories which, as a consequence efficiently hold exponentially more information than their classical counterparts. Finally, we present a quantum associative retrieval algorithm which can correct corrupted inputs and is exponentially faster than the Grover algorithm.
summary: We show that a quantum state can be perfectly cloned up to global mirroring with a unitary transformation that depends on one single parameter. We then show that this is equivalent to "perfect" cloning for quantum associative memories which, as a consequence efficiently hold exponentially more information than their classical counterparts. Finally, we present a quantum associative retrieval algorithm which can correct corrupted inputs and is exponentially faster than the Grover algorithm.
abstract_zh: 我们证明，量子态可以通过依赖于一个参数的酉变换完美地克隆到全局镜像。然后我们证明，这相当于量子联想记忆的“完美”克隆，因此它比经典记忆有效地保存了指数级更多的信息。最后，我们提出了一种量子关联检索算法，它可以纠正损坏的输入，并且比 Grover 算法快得多。

AI.tldr: 提出了一种基于镜像模克隆的量子联想检索方法，实现高效信息存储与快速检索。
AI.motivation: 为解决经典存储器信息容量有限和量子检索效率低下的问题，提升量子计算在信息处理中的应用潜力。
AI.method: 通过单参数酉变换实现量子态的镜像模克隆，并设计量子联想检索算法以纠正错误输入。
AI.result: 实现了量子态的完美镜像克隆，量子联想存储器信息容量指数级增长，且检索算法速度比Grover算法快指数级。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为量子信息存储与检索提供了新方法，展示了量子计算在处理复杂数据时的显著优势。

authors_text: Seyed Mostafa Kia,Nastaran Mohammadian Rad,Daniel van Opstal,Bart van Schie,Andre F. Marquand,Josien Pluim,Wiepke Cahn,Hugo G. Schnack
title: PROMISSING: Pruning Missing Values in Neural Networks
title_zh: 有前途：修剪神经网络中的缺失值
abstract: While data are the primary fuel for machine learning models, they often suffer from missing values, especially when collected in real-world scenarios. However, many off-the-shelf machine learning models, including artificial neural network models, are unable to handle these missing values directly. Therefore, extra data preprocessing and curation steps, such as data imputation, are inevitable before learning and prediction processes. In this study, we propose a simple and intuitive yet effective method for pruning missing values (PROMISSING) during learning and inference steps in neural networks. In this method, there is no need to remove or impute the missing values; instead, the missing values are treated as a new source of information (representing what we do not know). Our experiments on simulated data, several classification and regression benchmarks, and a multi-modal clinical dataset show that PROMISSING results in similar prediction performance compared to various imputation techniques. In addition, our experiments show models trained using PROMISSING techniques are becoming less decisive in their predictions when facing incomplete samples with many unknowns. This finding hopefully advances machine learning models from being pure predicting machines to more realistic thinkers that can also say "I do not know" when facing incomplete sources of information.
summary: While data are the primary fuel for machine learning models, they often suffer from missing values, especially when collected in real-world scenarios. However, many off-the-shelf machine learning models, including artificial neural network models, are unable to handle these missing values directly. Therefore, extra data preprocessing and curation steps, such as data imputation, are inevitable before learning and prediction processes. In this study, we propose a simple and intuitive yet effective method for pruning missing values (PROMISSING) during learning and inference steps in neural networks. In this method, there is no need to remove or impute the missing values; instead, the missing values are treated as a new source of information (representing what we do not know). Our experiments on simulated data, several classification and regression benchmarks, and a multi-modal clinical dataset show that PROMISSING results in similar prediction performance compared to various imputation techniques. In addition, our experiments show models trained using PROMISSING techniques are becoming less decisive in their predictions when facing incomplete samples with many unknowns. This finding hopefully advances machine learning models from being pure predicting machines to more realistic thinkers that can also say "I do not know" when facing incomplete sources of information.
abstract_zh: 虽然数据是机器学习模型的主要燃料，但它们经常遭受缺失值的影响，尤其是在现实场景中收集时。然而，许多现成的机器学习模型，包括人工神经网络模型，无法直接处理这些缺失值。因此，在学习和预测过程之前，额外的数据预处理和管理步骤（例如数据插补）是不可避免的。在这项研究中，我们提出了一种简单直观但有效的方法，用于在神经网络的学习和推理步骤中修剪缺失值（PROMISSING）。在这种方法中，不需要删除或估算缺失值；相反，缺失值被视为新的信息源（代表我们不知道的内容）。我们对模拟数据、多个分类和回归基准以及多模态临床数据集进行的实验表明，与各种插补技术相比，PROMISSING 会产生相似的预测性能。此外，我们的实验表明，当面对具有许多未知数的不完整样本时，使用有希望的技术训练的模型在预测中变得不那么具有决定性。这一发现有望将机器学习模型从纯粹的预测机器提升为更现实的思考者，在面对不完整的信息源时也可以说“我不知道”。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种在神经网络学习与推理过程中直接处理缺失值的新方法PROMISSING，无需传统数据预处理步骤。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中数据常存在缺失值，传统机器学习模型需依赖数据插值等预处理手段，但这些方法可能无法准确反映数据不确定性。
AI.method: 将缺失值视为新型信息源，在神经网络中直接保留缺失标记，通过模型自身学习缺失模式而非依赖外部插值技术。
AI.result: 在模拟数据、分类回归基准测试及多模态临床数据集上，PROMISSING性能与插值方法相当，且使模型在面对不完整样本时输出更保守的预测结果。
AI.conclusion: 该方法使机器学习模型从单纯预测工具进化为可表达不确定性的智能系统，更贴近人类面对未知信息时的理性判断过程。

authors_text: Alex Szorkovszky,Frank Veenstra,Kyrre Glette
title: Rapid rhythmic entrainment in bio-inspired central pattern generators
title_zh: 仿生中央模式发生器中的快速节奏夹带
comment: 7 pages, 6 figures. To appear in Proceedings of the IEEE International Joint Conference on Neural Networks 2022
comments: 7 pages, 6 figures. To appear in Proceedings of the IEEE International Joint Conference on Neural Networks 2022
abstract: Entrainment of movement to a periodic stimulus is a characteristic intelligent behaviour in humans and an important goal for adaptive robotics. We demonstrate a quadruped central pattern generator (CPG), consisting of modified Matsuoka neurons, that spontaneously adjusts its period of oscillation to that of a periodic input signal. This is done by simple forcing, with the aid of a filtering network as well as a neural model with tonic input-dependent oscillation period. We first use the NSGA3 algorithm to evolve the CPG parameters, using separate fitness functions for period tunability, limb homogeneity and gait stability. Four CPGs, maximizing different weighted averages of the fitness functions, are then selected from the Pareto front and each is used as a basis for optimizing a filter network. Different numbers of neurons are tested for each filter network. We find that period tunability in particular facilitates robust entrainment, that bounding gaits entrain more easily than walking gaits, and that more neurons in the filter network are beneficial for pre-processing input signals. The system that we present can be used in conjunction with sensory feedback to allow low-level adaptive and robust behaviour in walking robots.
summary: Entrainment of movement to a periodic stimulus is a characteristic intelligent behaviour in humans and an important goal for adaptive robotics. We demonstrate a quadruped central pattern generator (CPG), consisting of modified Matsuoka neurons, that spontaneously adjusts its period of oscillation to that of a periodic input signal. This is done by simple forcing, with the aid of a filtering network as well as a neural model with tonic input-dependent oscillation period. We first use the NSGA3 algorithm to evolve the CPG parameters, using separate fitness functions for period tunability, limb homogeneity and gait stability. Four CPGs, maximizing different weighted averages of the fitness functions, are then selected from the Pareto front and each is used as a basis for optimizing a filter network. Different numbers of neurons are tested for each filter network. We find that period tunability in particular facilitates robust entrainment, that bounding gaits entrain more easily than walking gaits, and that more neurons in the filter network are beneficial for pre-processing input signals. The system that we present can be used in conjunction with sensory feedback to allow low-level adaptive and robust behaviour in walking robots.
abstract_zh: 将运动引入周期性刺激是人类特有的智能行为，也是自适应机器人的重要目标。我们展示了一个四足中央模式发生器（CPG），它由改良的松冈神经元组成，可以自发地将其振荡周期调整为周期性输入信号的振荡周期。这是通过简单的强制，借助过滤网络以及具有依赖于强直输入的振荡周期的神经模型来完成的。我们首先使用 NSGA3 算法来演化 CPG 参数，使用单独的适应度函数来实现周期可调性、肢体均匀性和步态稳定性。然后从 Pareto 前沿选择四个 CPG，最大化适应度函数的不同加权平均值，并将每个 CPG 用作优化过滤网络的基础。每个过滤网络测试不同数量的神经元。我们发现周期可调性特别有利于鲁棒夹带，跳跃步态比行走步态更容易夹带，并且滤波器网络中更多的神经元有利于预处理输入信号。我们提出的系统可以与感觉反馈结合使用，以允许步行机器人具有低水平的自适应和鲁棒行为。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生物启发的中央模式发生器（CPG），使四足机器人能够快速适应周期性刺激的节奏。
AI.motivation: 人类能将运动与周期性刺激同步，这一能力对开发自适应机器人具有重要意义。
AI.method: 采用改进的Matsuoka神经元构建CPG，结合NSGA3算法优化参数，并通过滤波网络处理输入信号。
AI.result: 发现周期可调性显著提升同步鲁棒性，步态限制动作比行走动作更易同步，滤波网络神经元数量增加有助于信号预处理。
AI.conclusion: 该系统可与感官反馈结合，实现行走机器人低层级的自适应鲁棒控制。

authors_text: Danny Driess,Ingmar Schubert,Pete Florence,Yunzhu Li,Marc Toussaint
title: Reinforcement Learning with Neural Radiance Fields
title_zh: 神经辐射场的强化学习
abstract: It is a long-standing problem to find effective representations for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents. This paper demonstrates that learning state representations with supervision from Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) can improve the performance of RL compared to other learned representations or even low-dimensional, hand-engineered state information. Specifically, we propose to train an encoder that maps multiple image observations to a latent space describing the objects in the scene. The decoder built from a latent-conditioned NeRF serves as the supervision signal to learn the latent space. An RL algorithm then operates on the learned latent space as its state representation. We call this NeRF-RL. Our experiments indicate that NeRF as supervision leads to a latent space better suited for the downstream RL tasks involving robotic object manipulations like hanging mugs on hooks, pushing objects, or opening doors. Video: https://dannydriess.github.io/nerf-rl
summary: It is a long-standing problem to find effective representations for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents. This paper demonstrates that learning state representations with supervision from Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) can improve the performance of RL compared to other learned representations or even low-dimensional, hand-engineered state information. Specifically, we propose to train an encoder that maps multiple image observations to a latent space describing the objects in the scene. The decoder built from a latent-conditioned NeRF serves as the supervision signal to learn the latent space. An RL algorithm then operates on the learned latent space as its state representation. We call this NeRF-RL. Our experiments indicate that NeRF as supervision leads to a latent space better suited for the downstream RL tasks involving robotic object manipulations like hanging mugs on hooks, pushing objects, or opening doors. Video: https://dannydriess.github.io/nerf-rl
abstract_zh: 寻找有效的表征来训练强化学习（RL）代理是一个长期存在的问题。本文证明，与其他学习表示甚至低维、手工设计的状态信息相比，在神经辐射场 (NeRF) 的监督下学习状态表示可以提高 RL 的性能。具体来说，我们建议训练一个编码器，将多个图像观察结果映射到描述场景中对象的潜在空间。由潜在条件 NeRF 构建的解码器用作学习潜在空间的监督信号。然后，RL 算法对学习到的潜在空间进行操作作为其状态表示。我们称之为 NeRF-RL。我们的实验表明，NeRF 作为监督会产生一个更适合下游 RL 任务的潜在空间，这些任务涉及机器人对象操作，例如将杯子挂在钩子上、推动对象或打开门。视频：https://dannydriess.github.io/nerf-rl

AI.tldr: 提出利用神经辐射场（NeRF）作为监督信号来学习强化学习（RL）中的状态表示，以提升机器人操作任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统RL状态表示方法效果有限，而NeRF能提供更丰富的场景信息，有助于提升RL性能。
AI.method: 训练编码器将多视角图像映射到潜在空间，利用条件NeRF解码器作为监督信号，使RL算法在潜在空间中进行状态表示学习。
AI.result: 实验表明，NeRF监督下的潜在空间在机器人操作任务（如挂杯子、推物、开门）中显著提升RL性能。
AI.conclusion: NeRF作为监督信号能有效学习适合下游RL任务的潜在空间，提升机器人操作任务的性能。

search_text: Reinforcement Learning with Neural Radiance Fields 神经辐射场的强化学习 It is a long-standing problem to find effective representations for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents. This paper demonstrates that learning state representations with supervision from Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) can improve the performance of RL compared to other learned representations or even low-dimensional, hand-engineered state information. Specifically, we propose to train an encoder that maps multiple image observations to a latent space describing the objects in the scene. The decoder built from a latent-conditioned NeRF serves as the supervision signal to learn the latent space. An RL algorithm then operates on the learned latent space as its state representation. We call this NeRF-RL. Our experiments indicate that NeRF as supervision leads to a latent space better suited for the downstream RL tasks involving robotic object manipulations like hanging mugs on hooks, pushing objects, or opening doors. Video: https://dannydriess.github.io/nerf-rl 寻找有效的表征来训练强化学习（RL）代理是一个长期存在的问题。本文证明，与其他学习表示甚至低维、手工设计的状态信息相比，在神经辐射场 (NeRF) 的监督下学习状态表示可以提高 RL 的性能。具体来说，我们建议训练一个编码器，将多个图像观察结果映射到描述场景中对象的潜在空间。由潜在条件 NeRF 构建的解码器用作学习潜在空间的监督信号。然后，RL 算法对学习到的潜在空间进行操作作为其状态表示。我们称之为 NeRF-RL。我们的实验表明，NeRF 作为监督会产生一个更适合下游 RL 任务的潜在空间，这些任务涉及机器人对象操作，例如将杯子挂在钩子上、推动对象或打开门。视频：https://dannydriess.github.io/nerf-rl 提出利用神经辐射场（NeRF）作为监督信号来学习强化学习（RL）中的状态表示，以提升机器人操作任务的性能。 Danny Driess,Ingmar Schubert,Pete Florence,Yunzhu Li,Marc Toussaint cs.LG,cs.CV,cs.RO

authors_text: Chris Hamblin,Talia Konkle,George Alvarez
title: Pruning for Feature-Preserving Circuits in CNNs
title_zh: CNN 中保留特征电路的修剪
abstract: Deep convolutional neural networks are a powerful model class for a range of computer vision problems, but it is difficult to interpret the image filtering process they implement, given their sheer size. In this work, we introduce a method for extracting 'feature-preserving circuits' from deep CNNs, leveraging methods from saliency-based neural network pruning. These circuits are modular sub-functions, embedded within the network, containing only a subset of convolutional kernels relevant to a target feature. We compare the efficacy of 3 saliency-criteria for extracting these sparse circuits. Further, we show how 'sub-feature' circuits can be extracted, that preserve a feature's responses to particular images, dividing the feature into even sparser filtering processes. We also develop a tool for visualizing 'circuit diagrams', which render the entire image filtering process implemented by circuits in a parsable format.
summary: Deep convolutional neural networks are a powerful model class for a range of computer vision problems, but it is difficult to interpret the image filtering process they implement, given their sheer size. In this work, we introduce a method for extracting 'feature-preserving circuits' from deep CNNs, leveraging methods from saliency-based neural network pruning. These circuits are modular sub-functions, embedded within the network, containing only a subset of convolutional kernels relevant to a target feature. We compare the efficacy of 3 saliency-criteria for extracting these sparse circuits. Further, we show how 'sub-feature' circuits can be extracted, that preserve a feature's responses to particular images, dividing the feature into even sparser filtering processes. We also develop a tool for visualizing 'circuit diagrams', which render the entire image filtering process implemented by circuits in a parsable format.
abstract_zh: 深度卷积神经网络是解决一系列计算机视觉问题的强大模型类，但鉴于其庞大的规模，很难解释它们实现的图像过滤过程。在这项工作中，我们介绍了一种从深度 CNN 中提取“特征保留电路”的方法，利用基于显着性的神经网络修剪方法。这些电路是嵌入在网络中的模块化子功能，仅包含与目标特征相关的卷积核的子集。我们比较了提取这些稀疏电路的 3 个显着性标准的功效。此外，我们展示了如何提取“子特征”电路，保留特征对特定图像的响应，将特征划分为更稀疏的过滤过程。我们还开发了一种用于可视化“电路图”的工具，该工具以可解析的格式呈现由电路实现的整个图像过滤过程。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从深度卷积神经网络中提取'特征保留电路'的方法，通过显著性驱动的剪枝技术实现对CNN图像过滤过程的模块化解释。
AI.motivation: 深度卷积神经网络虽性能强大，但其庞大的规模导致图像过滤过程难以解释，亟需开发可提取可解释子模块的技术以增强模型透明度。
AI.method: 基于显著性分析的神经网络剪枝方法，比较三种显著性标准提取稀疏电路；进一步分解特征响应提取子特征电路，并开发可视化工具生成电路图。
AI.result: 验证了三种显著性标准在提取特征保留电路中的有效性，实现了特征响应的细粒度分解，可视化工具可清晰呈现电路级图像过滤过程。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过模块化子功能提取为CNN的可解释性研究提供了新思路，可视化工具为分析深度模型内部机制提供了实用技术手段。

authors_text: Rishabh Agarwal,Max Schwarzer,Pablo Samuel Castro,Aaron Courville,Marc G. Bellemare
title: Reincarnating Reinforcement Learning: Reusing Prior Computation to Accelerate Progress
title_zh: 强化学习的转世：重用先前的计算来加速进步
comment: NeurIPS 2022. Code and agents at https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl
comments: NeurIPS 2022. Code and agents at https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl
abstract: Learning tabula rasa, that is without any prior knowledge, is the prevalent workflow in reinforcement learning (RL) research. However, RL systems, when applied to large-scale settings, rarely operate tabula rasa. Such large-scale systems undergo multiple design or algorithmic changes during their development cycle and use ad hoc approaches for incorporating these changes without re-training from scratch, which would have been prohibitively expensive. Additionally, the inefficiency of deep RL typically excludes researchers without access to industrial-scale resources from tackling computationally-demanding problems. To address these issues, we present reincarnating RL as an alternative workflow or class of problem settings, where prior computational work (e.g., learned policies) is reused or transferred between design iterations of an RL agent, or from one RL agent to another. As a step towards enabling reincarnating RL from any agent to any other agent, we focus on the specific setting of efficiently transferring an existing sub-optimal policy to a standalone value-based RL agent. We find that existing approaches fail in this setting and propose a simple algorithm to address their limitations. Equipped with this algorithm, we demonstrate reincarnating RL's gains over tabula rasa RL on Atari 2600 games, a challenging locomotion task, and the real-world problem of navigating stratospheric balloons. Overall, this work argues for an alternative approach to RL research, which we believe could significantly improve real-world RL adoption and help democratize it further. Open-sourced code and trained agents at https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl.
summary: Learning tabula rasa, that is without any prior knowledge, is the prevalent workflow in reinforcement learning (RL) research. However, RL systems, when applied to large-scale settings, rarely operate tabula rasa. Such large-scale systems undergo multiple design or algorithmic changes during their development cycle and use ad hoc approaches for incorporating these changes without re-training from scratch, which would have been prohibitively expensive. Additionally, the inefficiency of deep RL typically excludes researchers without access to industrial-scale resources from tackling computationally-demanding problems. To address these issues, we present reincarnating RL as an alternative workflow or class of problem settings, where prior computational work (e.g., learned policies) is reused or transferred between design iterations of an RL agent, or from one RL agent to another. As a step towards enabling reincarnating RL from any agent to any other agent, we focus on the specific setting of efficiently transferring an existing sub-optimal policy to a standalone value-based RL agent. We find that existing approaches fail in this setting and propose a simple algorithm to address their limitations. Equipped with this algorithm, we demonstrate reincarnating RL's gains over tabula rasa RL on Atari 2600 games, a challenging locomotion task, and the real-world problem of navigating stratospheric balloons. Overall, this work argues for an alternative approach to RL research, which we believe could significantly improve real-world RL adoption and help democratize it further. Open-sourced code and trained agents at https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl.
abstract_zh: 在没有任何先验知识的情况下学习白板是强化学习（RL）研究中普遍的工作流程。然而，当应用于大规模环境时，强化学习系统很少会在白纸上运行。此类大型系统在开发周期中会经历多次设计或算法更改，并使用临时方法来合并这些更改，而无需从头开始重新训练，而重新训练的成本高昂。此外，深度强化学习的低效率通常会导致无法获得工业规模资源的研究人员无法解决计算要求较高的问题。为了解决这些问题，我们将重生 RL 作为一种替代工作流程或问题设置类别，其中先前的计算工作（例如学习的策略）在 RL 代理的设计迭代之间重用或转移，或从一个 RL 代理转移到另一个 RL 代理。作为实现 RL 从任何代理重生到任何其他代理的一步，我们专注于将现有次优策略有效转移到独立的基于值的 RL 代理的特定设置。我们发现现有的方法在这种情况下失败了，并提出了一种简单的算法来解决它们的局限性。配备此算法后，我们在 Atari 2600 游戏、具有挑战性的运动任务以及平流层气球导航的现实问题上展示了 RL 相对于白板 RL 的转世收益。总的来说，这项工作提出了强化学习研究的替代方法，我们相信这种方法可以显着提高现实世界中强化学习的采用，并有助于进一步民主化。开源代码和训练有素的代理位于 https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为'重生强化学习'（Reincarnating RL）的新方法，通过复用先前计算结果（如已学习的策略）来加速强化学习过程，避免从零开始训练。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习（RL）通常从零开始训练，导致大规模系统开发中重复计算成本高昂，且深度RL的低效限制了资源有限的研究者解决复杂问题的能力。
AI.method: 研究聚焦于将现有次优策略高效转移到独立的价值型RL智能体中，提出了一种简单算法以克服现有方法的局限性。
AI.result: 在Atari 2600游戏、复杂运动任务及平流层气球导航等实际问题中，'重生RL'显著优于传统从零开始的RL方法。
AI.conclusion: 该工作倡导一种替代性RL研究范式，认为复用先验计算可显著提升RL在现实场景中的应用效率并推动其普及。

Equipped with this algorithm, we demonstrate reincarnating RL's gains over tabula rasa RL on Atari 2600 games, a challenging locomotion task, and the real-world problem of navigating stratospheric balloons. Overall, this work argues for an alternative approach to RL research, which we believe could significantly improve real-world RL adoption and help democratize it further. Open-sourced code and trained agents at https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl. 在没有任何先验知识的情况下学习白板是强化学习（RL）研究中普遍的工作流程。然而，当应用于大规模环境时，强化学习系统很少会在白纸上运行。此类大型系统在开发周期中会经历多次设计或算法更改，并使用临时方法来合并这些更改，而无需从头开始重新训练，而重新训练的成本高昂。此外，深度强化学习的低效率通常会导致无法获得工业规模资源的研究人员无法解决计算要求较高的问题。为了解决这些问题，我们将重生 RL 作为一种替代工作流程或问题设置类别，其中先前的计算工作（例如学习的策略）在 RL 代理的设计迭代之间重用或转移，或从一个 RL 代理转移到另一个 RL 代理。作为实现 RL 从任何代理重生到任何其他代理的一步，我们专注于将现有次优策略有效转移到独立的基于值的 RL 代理的特定设置。我们发现现有的方法在这种情况下失败了，并提出了一种简单的算法来解决它们的局限性。配备此算法后，我们在 Atari 2600 游戏、具有挑战性的运动任务以及平流层气球导航的现实问题上展示了 RL 相对于白板 RL 的转世收益。总的来说，这项工作提出了强化学习研究的替代方法，我们相信这种方法可以显着提高现实世界中强化学习的采用，并有助于进一步民主化。开源代码和训练有素的代理位于 https://agarwl.github.io/reincarnating_rl。 本文提出了一种名为'重生强化学习'（Reincarnating RL）的新方法，通过复用先前计算结果（如已学习的策略）来加速强化学习过程，避免从零开始训练。 Rishabh Agarwal,Max Schwarzer,Pablo Samuel Castro,Aaron Courville,Marc G. Bellemare cs.LG,cs.AI,stat.ML

authors_text: Andrew Cropper,Céline Hocquette
title: Learning logic programs by combining programs
title_zh: 通过组合程序来学习逻辑程序
abstract: The goal of inductive logic programming is to induce a logic program (a set of logical rules) that generalises training examples. Inducing programs with many rules and literals is a major challenge. To tackle this challenge, we introduce an approach where we learn small non-separable programs and combine them. We implement our approach in a constraint-driven ILP system. Our approach can learn optimal and recursive programs and perform predicate invention. Our experiments on multiple domains, including game playing and program synthesis, show that our approach can drastically outperform existing approaches in terms of predictive accuracies and learning times, sometimes reducing learning times from over an hour to a few seconds.
summary: The goal of inductive logic programming is to induce a logic program (a set of logical rules) that generalises training examples. Inducing programs with many rules and literals is a major challenge. To tackle this challenge, we introduce an approach where we learn small non-separable programs and combine them. We implement our approach in a constraint-driven ILP system. Our approach can learn optimal and recursive programs and perform predicate invention. Our experiments on multiple domains, including game playing and program synthesis, show that our approach can drastically outperform existing approaches in terms of predictive accuracies and learning times, sometimes reducing learning times from over an hour to a few seconds.
abstract_zh: 归纳逻辑编程的目标是归纳出概括训练示例的逻辑程序（一组逻辑规则）。诱导具有许多规则和文字的程序是一个重大挑战。为了应对这一挑战，我们引入了一种学习小型不可分离程序并将它们组合起来的方法。我们在约束驱动的 ILP 系统中实施我们的方法。我们的方法可以学习最优和递归程序并执行谓词发明。我们在多个领域（包括游戏和程序合成）的实验表明，我们的方法在预测精度和学习时间方面可以大大优于现有方法，有时将学习时间从一个多小时缩短到几秒钟。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过组合小规模非分离逻辑程序来解决归纳逻辑编程中复杂程序学习的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有归纳逻辑编程方法在处理包含大量规则和文字的复杂程序时面临效率低下和准确率不足的问题。
AI.method: 提出一种基于约束驱动的归纳逻辑编程系统，通过组合多个小型非分离逻辑程序来学习复杂逻辑规则，并引入谓词发明机制。
AI.result: 在多个领域实验中，该方法在预测准确率和学习效率上显著优于现有方法，部分案例学习时间从小时级缩短至数秒。
AI.conclusion: 组合学习方法有效克服了复杂逻辑程序学习的挑战，为归纳逻辑编程提供了高效且准确的解决方案。

authors_text: Wenzhuo Yang,Hung Le,Tanmay Laud,Silvio Savarese,Steven C. H. Hoi
title: OmniXAI: A Library for Explainable AI
title_zh: OmniXAI：可解释的 AI 库
abstract: We introduce OmniXAI (short for Omni eXplainable AI), an open-source Python library of eXplainable AI (XAI), which offers omni-way explainable AI capabilities and various interpretable machine learning techniques to address the pain points of understanding and interpreting the decisions made by machine learning (ML) in practice. OmniXAI aims to be a one-stop comprehensive library that makes explainable AI easy for data scientists, ML researchers and practitioners who need explanation for various types of data, models and explanation methods at different stages of ML process (data exploration, feature engineering, model development, evaluation, and decision-making, etc). In particular, our library includes a rich family of explanation methods integrated in a unified interface, which supports multiple data types (tabular data, images, texts, time-series), multiple types of ML models (traditional ML in Scikit-learn and deep learning models in PyTorch/TensorFlow), and a range of diverse explanation methods including "model-specific" and "model-agnostic" ones (such as feature-attribution explanation, counterfactual explanation, gradient-based explanation, etc). For practitioners, the library provides an easy-to-use unified interface to generate the explanations for their applications by only writing a few lines of codes, and also a GUI dashboard for visualization of different explanations for more insights about decisions. In this technical report, we present OmniXAI's design principles, system architectures, and major functionalities, and also demonstrate several example use cases across different types of data, tasks, and models.
summary: We introduce OmniXAI (short for Omni eXplainable AI), an open-source Python library of eXplainable AI (XAI), which offers omni-way explainable AI capabilities and various interpretable machine learning techniques to address the pain points of understanding and interpreting the decisions made by machine learning (ML) in practice. OmniXAI aims to be a one-stop comprehensive library that makes explainable AI easy for data scientists, ML researchers and practitioners who need explanation for various types of data, models and explanation methods at different stages of ML process (data exploration, feature engineering, model development, evaluation, and decision-making, etc). In particular, our library includes a rich family of explanation methods integrated in a unified interface, which supports multiple data types (tabular data, images, texts, time-series), multiple types of ML models (traditional ML in Scikit-learn and deep learning models in PyTorch/TensorFlow), and a range of diverse explanation methods including "model-specific" and "model-agnostic" ones (such as feature-attribution explanation, counterfactual explanation, gradient-based explanation, etc). For practitioners, the library provides an easy-to-use unified interface to generate the explanations for their applications by only writing a few lines of codes, and also a GUI dashboard for visualization of different explanations for more insights about decisions. In this technical report, we present OmniXAI's design principles, system architectures, and major functionalities, and also demonstrate several example use cases across different types of data, tasks, and models.
abstract_zh: 我们介绍OmniXAI（Omni eXplainable AI的缩写），这是一个eXplainable AI（XAI）的开源Python库，它提供全方位可解释的AI能力和各种可解释的机器学习技术，以解决实践中理解和解释机器学习（ML）决策的痛点。 OmniXAI 旨在成为一个一站式综合库，为数据科学家、机器学习研究人员和从业者提供可解释的人工智能，帮助他们在机器学习过程的不同阶段（数据探索、特征工程、模型开发、评估和决策等）需要解释各种类型的数据、模型和解释方法。特别是，我们的库包含集成在统一接口中的丰富解释方法，支持多种数据类型（表格数据、图像、文本、时间序列）、多种类型的机器学习模型（Scikit-learn 中的传统机器学习和 PyTorch/TensorFlow 中的深度学习模型）以及一系列不同的解释方法，包括“特定于模型”和“模型无关”的解释方法（例如特征归因解释、反事实解释、基于梯度的解释、等）。对于从业者来说，该库提供了一个易于使用的统一界面，只需编写几行代码即可为其应用程序生成解释，并且还提供了一个 GUI 仪表板，用于可视化不同的解释，以获得有关决策的更多见解。在这份技术报告中，我们介绍了 OmniXAI 的设计原理、系统架构和主要功能，并演示了跨不同类型的数据、任务和模型的几个示例用例。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为OmniXAI的开源Python库，旨在提供全面的可解释人工智能（XAI）功能，解决机器学习模型决策解释的痛点。
AI.motivation: 当前机器学习模型的决策过程缺乏透明度，导致数据科学家和研究人员难以理解和解释模型行为，亟需一个统一的工具来支持多种数据类型、模型和解释方法。
AI.method: OmniXAI通过统一接口集成多种解释方法（如特征归因、反事实解释等），支持表格数据、图像、文本和时间序列等多种数据类型，兼容传统机器学习和深度学习模型，并提供可视化仪表板辅助分析。
AI.result: 该库通过多个跨数据类型、任务和模型的示例用例，验证了其在不同阶段（如数据探索、模型评估等）的实用性和易用性。
AI.conclusion: OmniXAI为数据科学家和研究人员提供了一站式解决方案，简化了可解释AI的实现过程，推动了可解释性在实际应用中的普及。

authors_text: Carlos Muli,Sangyoung Park,Mingming Liu
title: A Comparative Study on Energy Consumption Models for Drones
title_zh: 无人机能耗模型比较研究
comment: Accepted at Global IoT Summit 2022 Conference
comments: Accepted at Global IoT Summit 2022 Conference
abstract: Creating an appropriate energy consumption prediction model is becoming an important topic for drone-related research in the literature. However, a general consensus on the energy consumption model is yet to be reached at present. As a result, there are many variations that attempt to create models that range in complexity with a focus on different aspects. In this paper, we benchmark the five most popular energy consumption models for drones derived from their physical behaviours and point to the difficulties in matching with a realistic energy dataset collected from a delivery drone in flight under different testing conditions. Moreover, we propose a novel data-driven energy model using the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) based deep learning architecture and the accuracy is compared based on the dataset. Our experimental results have shown that the LSTM based approach can easily outperform other mathematical models for the dataset under study. Finally, sensitivity analysis has been carried out in order to interpret the model.
summary: Creating an appropriate energy consumption prediction model is becoming an important topic for drone-related research in the literature. However, a general consensus on the energy consumption model is yet to be reached at present. As a result, there are many variations that attempt to create models that range in complexity with a focus on different aspects. In this paper, we benchmark the five most popular energy consumption models for drones derived from their physical behaviours and point to the difficulties in matching with a realistic energy dataset collected from a delivery drone in flight under different testing conditions. Moreover, we propose a novel data-driven energy model using the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) based deep learning architecture and the accuracy is compared based on the dataset. Our experimental results have shown that the LSTM based approach can easily outperform other mathematical models for the dataset under study. Finally, sensitivity analysis has been carried out in order to interpret the model.
abstract_zh: 创建合适的能耗预测模型正在成为无人机相关研究的重要课题。但目前能源消费模式尚未达成普遍共识。因此，有许多变体试图创建复杂程度各异且侧重于不同方面的模型。在本文中，我们对根据其物理行为得出的五种最流行的无人机能耗模型进行了基准测试，并指出了与在不同测试条件下飞行中的送货无人机收集的实际能源数据集相匹配的困难。此外，我们提出了一种使用基于长短期记忆（LSTM）的深度学习架构的新型数据驱动能源模型，并根据数据集比较了准确性。我们的实验结果表明，对于所研究的数据集，基于 LSTM 的方法可以轻松优于其他数学模型。最后，进行了敏感性分析以解释该模型。

AI.tldr: 比较无人机能耗模型并提出基于LSTM的数据驱动模型
AI.motivation: 现有无人机能耗模型缺乏统一标准，需通过基准测试和新型模型提升预测准确性
AI.method: 基准测试五类物理行为模型，收集实际飞行数据，构建LSTM深度学习模型进行对比实验
AI.result: LSTM模型在测试数据集上显著优于传统数学模型
AI.conclusion: 数据驱动模型在复杂条件下更有效，但需进一步验证泛化能力

search_text: A Comparative Study on Energy Consumption Models for Drones 无人机能耗模型比较研究 Creating an appropriate energy consumption prediction model is becoming an important topic for drone-related research in the literature. However, a general consensus on the energy consumption model is yet to be reached at present. As a result, there are many variations that attempt to create models that range in complexity with a focus on different aspects. In this paper, we benchmark the five most popular energy consumption models for drones derived from their physical behaviours and point to the difficulties in matching with a realistic energy dataset collected from a delivery drone in flight under different testing conditions. Moreover, we propose a novel data-driven energy model using the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) based deep learning architecture and the accuracy is compared based on the dataset. Our experimental results have shown that the LSTM based approach can easily outperform other mathematical models for the dataset under study. Finally, sensitivity analysis has been carried out in order to interpret the model. 创建合适的能耗预测模型正在成为无人机相关研究的重要课题。但目前能源消费模式尚未达成普遍共识。因此，有许多变体试图创建复杂程度各异且侧重于不同方面的模型。在本文中，我们对根据其物理行为得出的五种最流行的无人机能耗模型进行了基准测试，并指出了与在不同测试条件下飞行中的送货无人机收集的实际能源数据集相匹配的困难。此外，我们提出了一种使用基于长短期记忆（LSTM）的深度学习架构的新型数据驱动能源模型，并根据数据集比较了准确性。我们的实验结果表明，对于所研究的数据集，基于 LSTM 的方法可以轻松优于其他数学模型。最后，进行了敏感性分析以解释该模型。 比较无人机能耗模型并提出基于LSTM的数据驱动模型 Carlos Muli,Sangyoung Park,Mingming Liu cs.RO,cs.LG

authors_text: Scott Larter,Rodrigo Queiroz,Sean Sedwards,Atrisha Sarkar,Krzysztof Czarnecki
title: A Hierarchical Pedestrian Behavior Model to Generate Realistic Human Behavior in Traffic Simulation
title_zh: 在交通模拟中生成真实人类行为的分层行人行为模型
comment: 9 pages, 4 figures, 3 tables. Accepted to the 2022 IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium
comments: 9 pages, 4 figures, 3 tables. Accepted to the 2022 IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium
abstract: Modelling pedestrian behavior is crucial in the development and testing of autonomous vehicles. In this work, we present a hierarchical pedestrian behavior model that generates high-level decisions through the use of behavior trees, in order to produce maneuvers executed by a low-level motion planner using an adapted Social Force model. A full implementation of our work is integrated into GeoScenario Server, a scenario definition and execution engine, extending its vehicle simulation capabilities with pedestrian simulation. The extended environment allows simulating test scenarios involving both vehicles and pedestrians to assist in the scenario-based testing process of autonomous vehicles. The presented hierarchical model is evaluated on two real-world data sets collected at separate locations with different road structures. Our model is shown to replicate the real-world pedestrians' trajectories with a high degree of fidelity and a decision-making accuracy of 98% or better, given only high-level routing information for each pedestrian.
summary: Modelling pedestrian behavior is crucial in the development and testing of autonomous vehicles. In this work, we present a hierarchical pedestrian behavior model that generates high-level decisions through the use of behavior trees, in order to produce maneuvers executed by a low-level motion planner using an adapted Social Force model. A full implementation of our work is integrated into GeoScenario Server, a scenario definition and execution engine, extending its vehicle simulation capabilities with pedestrian simulation. The extended environment allows simulating test scenarios involving both vehicles and pedestrians to assist in the scenario-based testing process of autonomous vehicles. The presented hierarchical model is evaluated on two real-world data sets collected at separate locations with different road structures. Our model is shown to replicate the real-world pedestrians' trajectories with a high degree of fidelity and a decision-making accuracy of 98% or better, given only high-level routing information for each pedestrian.
abstract_zh: 行人行为建模对于自动驾驶汽车的开发和测试至关重要。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个分层行人行为模型，该模型通过使用行为树生成高级决策，以便使用适应的社会力模型产生由低级运动规划器执行的动作。我们工作的完整实施已集成到场景定义和执行引擎 GeoScenario Server 中，通过行人模拟扩展其车辆模拟功能。扩展环境允许模拟涉及车辆和行人的测试场景，以协助自动驾驶汽车的基于场景的测试过程。所提出的分层模型是根据在不同位置、不同道路结构收集的两个真实数据集进行评估的。我们的模型能够以高保真度复制现实世界行人的轨迹，并且仅在给定每个行人的高级路线信息的情况下，决策准确度达到 98% 或更高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种分层行人行为模型，用于在交通模拟中生成逼真的人类行为，以增强自动驾驶车辆的测试场景。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆的开发和测试需要精确的行人行为模拟，以确保安全性和场景适应性。现有方法在复杂交互场景中存在不足，需改进模型真实性。
AI.method: 采用行为树生成高层决策，结合改进的Social Force模型实现低层运动规划，并集成至GeoScenario Server平台，支持车辆与行人协同模拟。
AI.result: 在两个真实场景数据集上验证，轨迹复现精度高，仅需高层路径信息即可达到98%的决策准确率。
AI.conclusion: 该模型有效提升了交通模拟中行人行为的真实性，为自动驾驶系统的场景化测试提供了可靠工具。

authors_text: Peize Li,Kaiwen Cai,Muhamad Risqi U. Saputra,Zhuangzhuang Dai,Chris Xiaoxuan Lu,Andrew Markham,Niki Trigoni
title: OdomBeyondVision: An Indoor Multi-modal Multi-platform Odometry Dataset Beyond the Visible Spectrum
title_zh: OdomBeyondVision：超越可见光谱的室内多模态多平台里程计数据集
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal indoor odometry dataset, OdomBeyondVision, featuring multiple sensors across the different spectrum and collected with different mobile platforms. Not only does OdomBeyondVision contain the traditional navigation sensors, sensors such as IMUs, mechanical LiDAR, RGBD camera, it also includes several emerging sensors such as the single-chip mmWave radar, LWIR thermal camera and solid-state LiDAR. With the above sensors on UAV, UGV and handheld platforms, we respectively recorded the multimodal odometry data and their movement trajectories in various indoor scenes and different illumination conditions. We release the exemplar radar, radar-inertial and thermal-inertial odometry implementations to demonstrate their results for future works to compare against and improve upon. The full dataset including toolkit and documentation is publicly available at: https://github.com/MAPS-Lab/OdomBeyondVision.
summary: This paper presents a multimodal indoor odometry dataset, OdomBeyondVision, featuring multiple sensors across the different spectrum and collected with different mobile platforms. Not only does OdomBeyondVision contain the traditional navigation sensors, sensors such as IMUs, mechanical LiDAR, RGBD camera, it also includes several emerging sensors such as the single-chip mmWave radar, LWIR thermal camera and solid-state LiDAR. With the above sensors on UAV, UGV and handheld platforms, we respectively recorded the multimodal odometry data and their movement trajectories in various indoor scenes and different illumination conditions. We release the exemplar radar, radar-inertial and thermal-inertial odometry implementations to demonstrate their results for future works to compare against and improve upon. The full dataset including toolkit and documentation is publicly available at: https://github.com/MAPS-Lab/OdomBeyondVision.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一个多模式室内里程计数据集 OdomBeyondVision，该数据集具有跨不同频谱的多个传感器，并通过不同的移动平台收集。 OdomBeyondVision不仅包含传统的导航传感器、IMU、机械激光雷达、RGBD相机等传感器，还包括单芯片毫米波雷达、长波红外热成像相机和固态激光雷达等多种新兴传感器。利用无人机、无人车和手持平台上的上述传感器，我们分别记录了各种室内场景和不同光照条件下的多模态测距数据及其运动轨迹。我们发布了示例雷达、雷达惯性和热惯性里程计实现，以展示其结果，供未来的工作进行比较和改进。包括工具包和文档的完整数据集可在以下网址公开获取：https://github.com/MAPS-Lab/OdomBeyondVision。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为OdomBeyondVision的室内多模态多平台里程计数据集，包含多种光谱传感器和不同移动平台的数据。
AI.motivation: 传统视觉传感器在复杂光照条件下性能受限，需探索非可见光谱传感器（如毫米波雷达、热成像）与多平台融合的里程计方案。
AI.method: 在无人机、无人车及手持设备上集成IMU、机械LiDAR、RGBD相机、毫米波雷达、热成像相机等传感器，采集多种室内场景及光照条件下的多模态里程计数据，并提供雷达-惯性、热-惯性里程计实现示例。
AI.result: 公开发布包含工具包和文档的完整数据集，提供基准算法实现供后续研究对比改进，支持多传感器融合的里程计方法验证。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集为非可见光谱多模态里程计研究提供基准，推动跨平台、多传感器融合算法的发展与应用验证。

With the above sensors on UAV, UGV and handheld platforms, we respectively recorded the multimodal odometry data and their movement trajectories in various indoor scenes and different illumination conditions. We release the exemplar radar, radar-inertial and thermal-inertial odometry implementations to demonstrate their results for future works to compare against and improve upon. The full dataset including toolkit and documentation is publicly available at: https://github.com/MAPS-Lab/OdomBeyondVision. 本文提出了一个多模式室内里程计数据集 OdomBeyondVision，该数据集具有跨不同频谱的多个传感器，并通过不同的移动平台收集。 OdomBeyondVision不仅包含传统的导航传感器、IMU、机械激光雷达、RGBD相机等传感器，还包括单芯片毫米波雷达、长波红外热成像相机和固态激光雷达等多种新兴传感器。利用无人机、无人车和手持平台上的上述传感器，我们分别记录了各种室内场景和不同光照条件下的多模态测距数据及其运动轨迹。我们发布了示例雷达、雷达惯性和热惯性里程计实现，以展示其结果，供未来的工作进行比较和改进。包括工具包和文档的完整数据集可在以下网址公开获取：https://github.com/MAPS-Lab/OdomBeyondVision。 本文提出一个名为OdomBeyondVision的室内多模态多平台里程计数据集，包含多种光谱传感器和不同移动平台的数据。 Peize Li,Kaiwen Cai,Muhamad Risqi U. Saputra,Zhuangzhuang Dai,Chris Xiaoxuan Lu,Andrew Markham,Niki Trigoni cs.RO

authors_text: Rhythm Arora,Matteo Lavit Nicora,Pooja Prajod,Daniele Panzeri,Elisabeth André,Patrick Gebhard,Matteo Malosio
title: Employing Socially Interactive Agents for Robotic Neurorehabilitation Training
title_zh: 使用社交互动代理进行机器人神经康复训练
comment: The 5th Workshop on Behavior Adaptation Interaction and Learning for Assistive Robotics (BAILAR)
comments: The 5th Workshop on Behavior Adaptation Interaction and Learning for Assistive Robotics (BAILAR)
abstract: In today's world, many patients with cognitive impairments and motor dysfunction seek the attention of experts to perform specific conventional therapies to improve their situation. However, due to a lack of neurorehabilitation professionals, patients suffer from severe effects that worsen their condition. In this paper, we present a technological approach for a novel robotic neurorehabilitation training system. It relies on a combination of a rehabilitation device, signal classification methods, supervised machine learning models for training adaptation, training exercises, and socially interactive agents as a user interface. Together with a professional, the system can be trained towards the patient's specific needs. Furthermore, after a training phase, patients are enabled to train independently at home without the assistance of a physical therapist with a socially interactive agent in the role of a coaching assistant.
summary: In today's world, many patients with cognitive impairments and motor dysfunction seek the attention of experts to perform specific conventional therapies to improve their situation. However, due to a lack of neurorehabilitation professionals, patients suffer from severe effects that worsen their condition. In this paper, we present a technological approach for a novel robotic neurorehabilitation training system. It relies on a combination of a rehabilitation device, signal classification methods, supervised machine learning models for training adaptation, training exercises, and socially interactive agents as a user interface. Together with a professional, the system can be trained towards the patient's specific needs. Furthermore, after a training phase, patients are enabled to train independently at home without the assistance of a physical therapist with a socially interactive agent in the role of a coaching assistant.
abstract_zh: 当今世界，许多患有认知障碍和运动功能障碍的患者寻求专家的关注，以进行特定的常规疗法来改善他们的状况。然而，由于缺乏神经康复专业人员，患者遭受严重影响，病情恶化。在本文中，我们提出了一种新型机器人神经康复训练系统的技术方法。它依赖于康复设备、信号分类方法、用于训练适应的监督机器学习模型、训练练习和作为用户界面的社交互动代理的组合。与专业人员一起，可以针对患者的特定需求对该系统进行培训。此外，训练阶段结束后，患者可以在家中独立训练，无需物理治疗师的帮助，并由社交互动代理担任教练助理。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合社交互动代理的机器人神经康复训练系统，以解决专业康复人员不足导致的患者治疗困境。
AI.motivation: 由于神经康复专业人员短缺，患者难以获得持续有效的康复训练，导致病情恶化。
AI.method: 整合康复设备、信号分类技术、监督学习模型及社交互动代理，构建可适应患者需求的训练系统，并支持家庭自主训练。
AI.result: 系统可由专业人员指导个性化训练，后续患者能通过社交互动代理在家独立完成康复练习。
AI.conclusion: 该技术方案有效缓解康复资源不足问题，提升患者训练可及性与康复效果。

authors_text: Xiliang Zhu,David Rossouw,Shayna Gardiner,Simon Corston-Oliver
title: Extracting Similar Questions From Naturally-occurring Business Conversations
title_zh: 从自然发生的业务对话中提取类似的问题
abstract: Pre-trained contextualized embedding models such as BERT are a standard building block in many natural language processing systems. We demonstrate that the sentence-level representations produced by some off-the-shelf contextualized embedding models have a narrow distribution in the embedding space, and thus perform poorly for the task of identifying semantically similar questions in real-world English business conversations. We describe a method that uses appropriately tuned representations and a small set of exemplars to group questions of interest to business users in a visualization that can be used for data exploration or employee coaching.
summary: Pre-trained contextualized embedding models such as BERT are a standard building block in many natural language processing systems. We demonstrate that the sentence-level representations produced by some off-the-shelf contextualized embedding models have a narrow distribution in the embedding space, and thus perform poorly for the task of identifying semantically similar questions in real-world English business conversations. We describe a method that uses appropriately tuned representations and a small set of exemplars to group questions of interest to business users in a visualization that can be used for data exploration or employee coaching.
abstract_zh: 预训练的上下文嵌入模型（例如 BERT）是许多自然语言处理系统中的标准构建块。我们证明，一些现成的上下文嵌入模型产生的句子级表示在嵌入空间中分布较窄，因此在识别现实英语商务对话中语义相似问题的任务中表现不佳。我们描述了一种方法，该方法使用适当调整的表示和一小组示例来将业务用户感兴趣的问题分组到可视化中，该可视化可用于数据探索或员工辅导。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进方法，解决预训练嵌入模型在商业对话中提取语义相似问题时表现不佳的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有预训练模型（如BERT）生成的句子表示分布狭窄，难以准确识别真实商业对话中的语义相似问题。
AI.method: 通过调整嵌入表示并结合少量示例，将用户关注的问题进行分组并生成可视化结果。
AI.result: 开发出可用于数据探索和员工培训的可视化工具，有效提升相似问题识别能力。
AI.conclusion: 传统嵌入模型存在局限性，而本文方法通过优化表示和示例利用，显著改善了商业场景下的问题分组效果。
