authors_text: Manuela Fischer,Jeff Giliberti,Christoph Grunau
title: Improved Deterministic Connectivity in Massively Parallel Computation
title_zh: 改进大规模并行计算中的确定性连接
abstract: A long line of research about connectivity in the Massively Parallel Computation model has culminated in the seminal works of Andoni et al. [FOCS'18] and Behnezhad et al. [FOCS'19]. They provide a randomized algorithm for low-space MPC with conjectured to be optimal round complexity $O(\log D + \log \log_{\frac m n} n)$ and $O(m)$ space, for graphs on $n$ vertices with $m$ edges and diameter $D$. Surprisingly, a recent result of Coy and Czumaj [STOC'22] shows how to achieve the same deterministically. Unfortunately, however, their algorithm suffers from large local computation time. We present a deterministic connectivity algorithm that matches all the parameters of the randomized algorithm and, in addition, significantly reduces the local computation time to nearly linear. Our derandomization method is based on reducing the amount of randomness needed to allow for a simpler efficient search. While similar randomness reduction approaches have been used before, our result is not only strikingly simpler, but it is the first to have efficient local computation. This is why we believe it to serve as a starting point for the systematic development of computation-efficient derandomization approaches in low-memory MPC.
summary: A long line of research about connectivity in the Massively Parallel Computation model has culminated in the seminal works of Andoni et al. [FOCS'18] and Behnezhad et al. [FOCS'19]. They provide a randomized algorithm for low-space MPC with conjectured to be optimal round complexity $O(\log D + \log \log_{\frac m n} n)$ and $O(m)$ space, for graphs on $n$ vertices with $m$ edges and diameter $D$. Surprisingly, a recent result of Coy and Czumaj [STOC'22] shows how to achieve the same deterministically. Unfortunately, however, their algorithm suffers from large local computation time. We present a deterministic connectivity algorithm that matches all the parameters of the randomized algorithm and, in addition, significantly reduces the local computation time to nearly linear. Our derandomization method is based on reducing the amount of randomness needed to allow for a simpler efficient search. While similar randomness reduction approaches have been used before, our result is not only strikingly simpler, but it is the first to have efficient local computation. This is why we believe it to serve as a starting point for the systematic development of computation-efficient derandomization approaches in low-memory MPC.
abstract_zh: 关于大规模并行计算模型中的连通性的一系列研究在 Andoni 等人的开创性工作中达到了顶峰。 [FOCS'18] 和 Behnezhad 等人。 [FOCS'19]。他们为低空间 MPC 提供了一种随机算法，对于具有 $m$ 边和直径 $D$ 的 $n$ 顶点上的图，推测最佳轮复杂度为 $O(\log D + \log \log_{\frac m n} n)$ 和 $O(m)$ 空间。令人惊讶的是，Coy 和 Czumaj [STOC'22] 最近的结果显示了如何确定性地实现相同的目标。然而不幸的是，他们的算法受到大量本地计算时间的影响。我们提出了一种确定性连接算法，它与随机算法的所有参数相匹配，此外，还显着地将本地计算时间减少到接近线性。我们的去随机化方法基于减少所需的随机量，以实现更简单有效的搜索。虽然之前已经使用过类似的随机性降低方法，但我们的结果不仅非常简单，而且是第一个具有高效本地计算的方法。这就是为什么我们相信它可以作为低内存 MPC 中计算高效的去随机化方法的系统开发的起点。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种改进的确定性连通性算法，用于大规模并行计算模型，该算法在保持与随机化算法相同参数的同时显著降低了本地计算时间。
AI.motivation: 尽管现有确定性算法能匹配随机化算法的轮复杂度和空间需求，但其本地计算时间过长，限制了实际应用。需要更高效的确定性解决方案。
AI.method: 通过减少所需随机性以简化高效搜索过程，采用新颖的随机性降低方法实现确定化，同时保持参数最优。
AI.result: 设计出首个本地计算时间接近线性的确定性连通性算法，在轮复杂度和空间需求上与随机化算法一致，且显著优化了计算效率。
AI.conclusion: 该成果为低内存MPC模型中系统化开发计算高效去随机化方法提供了理论基础和实现范式。

method: 通过减少所需随机性以简化高效搜索过程，采用新颖的随机性降低方法实现确定化，同时保持参数最优。
result: 设计出首个本地计算时间接近线性的确定性连通性算法，在轮复杂度和空间需求上与随机化算法一致，且显著优化了计算效率。
conclusion: 该成果为低内存MPC模型中系统化开发计算高效去随机化方法提供了理论基础和实现范式。
search_text: Improved Deterministic Connectivity in Massively Parallel Computation 改进大规模并行计算中的确定性连接 A long line of research about connectivity in the Massively Parallel Computation model has culminated in the seminal works of Andoni et al. [FOCS'18] and Behnezhad et al. [FOCS'19]. They provide a randomized algorithm for low-space MPC with conjectured to be optimal round complexity $O(\log D + \log \log_{\frac m n} n)$ and $O(m)$ space, for graphs on $n$ vertices with $m$ edges and diameter $D$. Surprisingly, a recent result of Coy and Czumaj [STOC'22] shows how to achieve the same deterministically. Unfortunately, however, their algorithm suffers from large local computation time. We present a deterministic connectivity algorithm that matches all the parameters of the randomized algorithm and, in addition, significantly reduces the local computation time to nearly linear. Our derandomization method is based on reducing the amount of randomness needed to allow for a simpler efficient search. While similar randomness reduction approaches have been used before, our result is not only strikingly simpler, but it is the first to have efficient local computation. This is why we believe it to serve as a starting point for the systematic development of computation-efficient derandomization approaches in low-memory MPC. 关于大规模并行计算模型中的连通性的一系列研究在 Andoni 等人的开创性工作中达到了顶峰。 [FOCS'18] 和 Behnezhad 等人。 [FOCS'19]。他们为低空间 MPC 提供了一种随机算法，对于具有 $m$ 边和直径 $D$ 的 $n$ 顶点上的图，推测最佳轮复杂度为 $O(\log D + \log \log_{\frac m n} n)$ 和 $O(m)$ 空间。令人惊讶的是，Coy 和 Czumaj [STOC'22] 最近的结果显示了如何确定性地实现相同的目标。然而不幸的是，他们的算法受到大量本地计算时间的影响。我们提出了一种确定性连接算法，它与随机算法的所有参数相匹配，此外，还显着地将本地计算时间减少到接近线性。我们的去随机化方法基于减少所需的随机量，以实现更简单有效的搜索。虽然之前已经使用过类似的随机性降低方法，但我们的结果不仅非常简单，而且是第一个具有高效本地计算的方法。这就是为什么我们相信它可以作为低内存 MPC 中计算高效的去随机化方法的系统开发的起点。 本文提出了一种改进的确定性连通性算法，用于大规模并行计算模型，该算法在保持与随机化算法相同参数的同时显著降低了本地计算时间。 Manuela Fischer,Jeff Giliberti,Christoph Grunau cs.DS,cs.DC

authors_text: Amaury Trujillo
title: Hyperownership: Beyond the Current State of Interaction with Digital Property
title_zh: 超级所有权：超越当前与数字财产互动的状态
comment: Accepted Blue Sky Idea in the 33rd ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media (HT '22)
comments: Accepted Blue Sky Idea in the 33rd ACM Conference on Hypertext and Social Media (HT '22)
abstract: The introduction of novel technology has oftentimes changed the concept of ownership. Non-fungible tokens are a recent example, as they allow a decentralized way to generate and verify proof of ownership via distributed ledger technology. Despite crucial uncertainties, these tokens have generated great enthusiasm for the future of digital property and its surrounding economy. In this regard, I think there is an untapped opportunity in applying a hypertext approach to augment such highly structured ownership-based associations. To this end, in this work I propose hyperownership, based on the premises that property is the law of lists and ledgers, and that hypertext is an apt method to inquiry such a ledger system. In spite of the significant risks and challenges to realize such a vision, I believe that it has great potential to transform the way with which we interact with digital property.
summary: The introduction of novel technology has oftentimes changed the concept of ownership. Non-fungible tokens are a recent example, as they allow a decentralized way to generate and verify proof of ownership via distributed ledger technology. Despite crucial uncertainties, these tokens have generated great enthusiasm for the future of digital property and its surrounding economy. In this regard, I think there is an untapped opportunity in applying a hypertext approach to augment such highly structured ownership-based associations. To this end, in this work I propose hyperownership, based on the premises that property is the law of lists and ledgers, and that hypertext is an apt method to inquiry such a ledger system. In spite of the significant risks and challenges to realize such a vision, I believe that it has great potential to transform the way with which we interact with digital property.
abstract_zh: 新技术的引入常常改变所有权的概念。不可替代的代币是最近的一个例子，因为它们允许通过分布式账本技术以去中心化的方式生成和验证所有权证明。尽管存在重大不确定性，但这些代币激发了人们对数字财产及其周边经济的未来的极大热情。在这方面，我认为应用超文本方法来增强这种高度结构化的基于所有权的关联有一个尚未开发的机会。为此，在这项工作中，我提出了超级所有权，其前提是财产是清单和分类帐的法则，并且超文本是查询这种分类帐系统的合适方法。尽管实现这一愿景面临重大风险和挑战，但我相信它具有改变我们与数字财产互动方式的巨大潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于超文本技术的新型数字财产所有权概念——超所有权（hyperownership），旨在通过增强所有权关联性来改进现有数字财产交互方式。
AI.motivation: 尽管NFT等技术推动了数字财产发展，但现有基于账本的产权系统存在局限性，需要更灵活的交互方式来应对数字财产经济的复杂性。
AI.method: 以'产权是列表与账本的法则'为前提，提出通过超文本结构拓展传统账本系统，建立更动态的所有权关联网络。
AI.result: 提出了超所有权理论框架，论证了其在增强数字财产交互性方面的潜力，但尚未实现具体技术实现。
AI.conclusion: 超所有权概念为数字财产交互提供了创新视角，尽管面临技术与制度挑战，但可能推动产权管理范式的革新。

authors_text: Reinald Kim Amplayo,Arthur Bražinskas,Yoshi Suhara,Xiaolan Wang,Bing Liu
title: Beyond Opinion Mining: Summarizing Opinions of Customer Reviews
title_zh: 超越意见挖掘：总结客户评论的意见
abstract: Customer reviews are vital for making purchasing decisions in the Information Age. Such reviews can be automatically summarized to provide the user with an overview of opinions. In this tutorial, we present various aspects of opinion summarization that are useful for researchers and practitioners. First, we will introduce the task and major challenges. Then, we will present existing opinion summarization solutions, both pre-neural and neural. We will discuss how summarizers can be trained in the unsupervised, few-shot, and supervised regimes. Each regime has roots in different machine learning methods, such as auto-encoding, controllable text generation, and variational inference. Finally, we will discuss resources and evaluation methods and conclude with the future directions. This three-hour tutorial will provide a comprehensive overview over major advances in opinion summarization. The listeners will be well-equipped with the knowledge that is both useful for research and practical applications.
summary: Customer reviews are vital for making purchasing decisions in the Information Age. Such reviews can be automatically summarized to provide the user with an overview of opinions. In this tutorial, we present various aspects of opinion summarization that are useful for researchers and practitioners. First, we will introduce the task and major challenges. Then, we will present existing opinion summarization solutions, both pre-neural and neural. We will discuss how summarizers can be trained in the unsupervised, few-shot, and supervised regimes. Each regime has roots in different machine learning methods, such as auto-encoding, controllable text generation, and variational inference. Finally, we will discuss resources and evaluation methods and conclude with the future directions. This three-hour tutorial will provide a comprehensive overview over major advances in opinion summarization. The listeners will be well-equipped with the knowledge that is both useful for research and practical applications.
abstract_zh: 在信息时代，客户评论对于做出购买决策至关重要。此类评论可以自动总结，以为用户提供意见概述。在本教程中，我们介绍了对研究人员和从业者有用的观点总结的各个方面。首先介绍一下任务和主要挑战。然后，我们将提出现有的意见总结解决方案，包括前神经和神经。我们将讨论如何在无监督、少样本和监督机制中训练摘要器。每种机制都源于不同的机器学习方法，例如自动编码、可控文本生成和变分推理。最后，我们将讨论资源和评估方法，并总结未来的方向。这个三小时的教程将全面概述意见总结方面的主要进展。听众将充分掌握对研究和实际应用都有用的知识。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过自动总结顾客评论中的意见，为用户提供购买决策的概览。
AI.motivation: 顾客评论在信息时代对购买决策至关重要，但手动总结耗时且低效，需自动化解决方案。
AI.method: 介绍传统与神经网络方法，涵盖无监督、少样本及监督学习训练方式，并结合自编码、可控生成等技术。
AI.result: 系统梳理了意见总结的主要进展，提供资源与评估方法，为研究者和实践者提供实用知识。
AI.conclusion: 未来需进一步探索更高效的方法，教程为研究与应用奠定了理论与实践基础。

authors_text: Edward L. Campbell,Judith Dineley,Pauline Conde,Faith Matcham,Femke Lamers,Sara Siddi,Laura Docio-Fernandez,Carmen Garcia-Mateo,Nicholas Cummins,the RADAR-CNS Consortium
title: Detecting the Severity of Major Depressive Disorder from Speech: A Novel HARD-Training Methodology
title_zh: 从言语中检测重度抑郁症的严重程度：一种新的艰苦训练方法
abstract: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) is a common worldwide mental health issue with high associated socioeconomic costs. The prediction and automatic detection of MDD can, therefore, make a huge impact on society. Speech, as a non-invasive, easy to collect signal, is a promising marker to aid the diagnosis and assessment of MDD. In this regard, speech samples were collected as part of the Remote Assessment of Disease and Relapse in Major Depressive Disorder (RADAR-MDD) research programme. RADAR-MDD was an observational cohort study in which speech and other digital biomarkers were collected from a cohort of individuals with a history of MDD in Spain, United Kingdom and the Netherlands. In this paper, the RADAR-MDD speech corpus was taken as an experimental framework to test the efficacy of a Sequence-to-Sequence model with a local attention mechanism in a two-class depression severity classification paradigm. Additionally, a novel training method, HARD-Training, is proposed. It is a methodology based on the selection of more ambiguous samples for the model training, and inspired by the curriculum learning paradigm. HARD-Training was found to consistently improve - with an average increment of 8.6% - the performance of our classifiers for both of two speech elicitation tasks used and each collection site of the RADAR-MDD speech corpus. With this novel methodology, our Sequence-to-Sequence model was able to effectively detect MDD severity regardless of language. Finally, recognising the need for greater awareness of potential algorithmic bias, we conduct an additional analysis of our results separately for each gender.
summary: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) is a common worldwide mental health issue with high associated socioeconomic costs. The prediction and automatic detection of MDD can, therefore, make a huge impact on society. Speech, as a non-invasive, easy to collect signal, is a promising marker to aid the diagnosis and assessment of MDD. In this regard, speech samples were collected as part of the Remote Assessment of Disease and Relapse in Major Depressive Disorder (RADAR-MDD) research programme. RADAR-MDD was an observational cohort study in which speech and other digital biomarkers were collected from a cohort of individuals with a history of MDD in Spain, United Kingdom and the Netherlands. In this paper, the RADAR-MDD speech corpus was taken as an experimental framework to test the efficacy of a Sequence-to-Sequence model with a local attention mechanism in a two-class depression severity classification paradigm. Additionally, a novel training method, HARD-Training, is proposed. It is a methodology based on the selection of more ambiguous samples for the model training, and inspired by the curriculum learning paradigm. HARD-Training was found to consistently improve - with an average increment of 8.6% - the performance of our classifiers for both of two speech elicitation tasks used and each collection site of the RADAR-MDD speech corpus. With this novel methodology, our Sequence-to-Sequence model was able to effectively detect MDD severity regardless of language. Finally, recognising the need for greater awareness of potential algorithmic bias, we conduct an additional analysis of our results separately for each gender.
abstract_zh: 重度抑郁症（MDD）是一种全球常见的心理健康问题，具有很高的相关社会经济成本。因此，MDD 的预测和自动检测可以对社会产生巨大影响。言语作为一种非侵入性、易于采集的信号，是一种有前途的辅助MDD诊断和评估的标志物。在这方面，收集了语音样本，作为重度抑郁症疾病和复发远程评估（RADAR-MDD）研究计划的一部分。 RADAR-MDD 是一项观察性队列研究，从西班牙、英国和荷兰有 MDD 病史的人群中收集了言语和其他数字生物标记物。本文以 RADAR-MDD 语音语料库为实验框架，测试具有局部注意机制的序列到序列模型在两级抑郁症严重程度分类范式中的有效性。此外，还提出了一种新的训练方法——HARD-Training。它是一种基于选择更模糊样本进行模型训练的方法，并受到课程学习范式的启发。研究发现，HARD-Training 持续改进了我们的分类器在所使用的两个语音启发任务和 RADAR-MDD 语音语料库的每个收集站点上的性能，平均增量为 8.6%。借助这种新颖的方法，我们的序列到序列模型能够有效检测 MDD 严重程度，无论语言如何。最后，认识到需要提高对潜在算法偏差的认识，我们对每个性别的结果分别进行了额外分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新型HARD-训练方法，利用语音数据检测重度抑郁症严重程度，并验证其跨语言有效性。
AI.motivation: 抑郁症具有高社会经济成本，语音作为非侵入性生物标志物可辅助诊断，但现有模型存在跨语言性能不足和算法偏见问题。
AI.method: 基于RADAR-MDD语音语料库，采用带局部注意力机制的序列到序列模型，提出HARD-训练方法（通过选择模糊样本进行训练，受课程学习启发）。
AI.result: HARD-训练使分类器性能平均提升8.6%，模型在西班牙、英国、荷兰三地数据及不同语言中均有效，且完成性别偏见分析。
AI.conclusion: HARD-训练方法显著提升抑郁症严重程度检测效果，同时通过多语言和性别分析增强了模型的公平性与普适性。

search_text: Detecting the Severity of Major Depressive Disorder from Speech: A Novel HARD-Training Methodology 从言语中检测重度抑郁症的严重程度：一种新的艰苦训练方法 Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) is a common worldwide mental health issue with high associated socioeconomic costs. The prediction and automatic detection of MDD can, therefore, make a huge impact on society. Speech, as a non-invasive, easy to collect signal, is a promising marker to aid the diagnosis and assessment of MDD. In this regard, speech samples were collected as part of the Remote Assessment of Disease and Relapse in Major Depressive Disorder (RADAR-MDD) research programme. RADAR-MDD was an observational cohort study in which speech and other digital biomarkers were collected from a cohort of individuals with a history of MDD in Spain, United Kingdom and the Netherlands. In this paper, the RADAR-MDD speech corpus was taken as an experimental framework to test the efficacy of a Sequence-to-Sequence model with a local attention mechanism in a two-class depression severity classification paradigm. Additionally, a novel training method, HARD-Training, is proposed. It is a methodology based on the selection of more ambiguous samples for the model training, and inspired by the curriculum learning paradigm. HARD-Training was found to consistently improve - with an average increment of 8.6% - the performance of our classifiers for both of two speech elicitation tasks used and each collection site of the RADAR-MDD speech corpus. With this novel methodology, our Sequence-to-Sequence model was able to effectively detect MDD severity regardless of language. Finally, recognising the need for greater awareness of potential algorithmic bias, we conduct an additional analysis of our results separately for each gender. 重度抑郁症（MDD）是一种全球常见的心理健康问题，具有很高的相关社会经济成本。因此，MDD 的预测和自动检测可以对社会产生巨大影响。言语作为一种非侵入性、易于采集的信号，是一种有前途的辅助MDD诊断和评估的标志物。在这方面，收集了语音样本，作为重度抑郁症疾病和复发远程评估（RADAR-MDD）研究计划的一部分。 RADAR-MDD 是一项观察性队列研究，从西班牙、英国和荷兰有 MDD 病史的人群中收集了言语和其他数字生物标记物。本文以 RADAR-MDD 语音语料库为实验框架，测试具有局部注意机制的序列到序列模型在两级抑郁症严重程度分类范式中的有效性。此外，还提出了一种新的训练方法——HARD-Training。它是一种基于选择更模糊样本进行模型训练的方法，并受到课程学习范式的启发。研究发现，HARD-Training 持续改进了我们的分类器在所使用的两个语音启发任务和 RADAR-MDD 语音语料库的每个收集站点上的性能，平均增量为 8.6%。借助这种新颖的方法，我们的序列到序列模型能够有效检测 MDD 严重程度，无论语言如何。最后，认识到需要提高对潜在算法偏差的认识，我们对每个性别的结果分别进行了额外分析。 本文提出一种新型HARD-训练方法，利用语音数据检测重度抑郁症严重程度，并验证其跨语言有效性。 Edward L. Campbell,Judith Dineley,Pauline Conde,Faith Matcham,Femke Lamers,Sara Siddi,Laura Docio-Fernandez,Carmen Garcia-Mateo,Nicholas Cummins,the RADAR-CNS Consortium cs.SD,cs.LG,eess.AS,q-bio.QM

authors_text: Yizhen Zheng,Shirui Pan,Vincent Cs Lee,Yu Zheng,Philip S. Yu
title: Rethinking and Scaling Up Graph Contrastive Learning: An Extremely Efficient Approach with Group Discrimination
title_zh: 重新思考和扩大图对比学习：一种极其有效的群体歧视方法
abstract: Graph contrastive learning (GCL) alleviates the heavy reliance on label information for graph representation learning (GRL) via self-supervised learning schemes. The core idea is to learn by maximising mutual information for similar instances, which requires similarity computation between two node instances. However, GCL is inefficient in both time and memory consumption. In addition, GCL normally requires a large number of training epochs to be well-trained on large-scale datasets. Inspired by an observation of a technical defect (i.e., inappropriate usage of Sigmoid function) commonly used in two representative GCL works, DGI and MVGRL, we revisit GCL and introduce a new learning paradigm for self-supervised graph representation learning, namely, Group Discrimination (GD), and propose a novel GD-based method called Graph Group Discrimination (GGD). Instead of similarity computation, GGD directly discriminates two groups of node samples with a very simple binary cross-entropy loss. In addition, GGD requires much fewer training epochs to obtain competitive performance compared with GCL methods on large-scale datasets. These two advantages endow GGD with very efficient property. Extensive experiments show that GGD outperforms state-of-the-art self-supervised methods on eight datasets. In particular, GGD can be trained in 0.18 seconds (6.44 seconds including data preprocessing) on ogbn-arxiv, which is orders of magnitude (10,000+) faster than GCL baselines while consuming much less memory. Trained with 9 hours on ogbn-papers100M with billion edges, GGD outperforms its GCL counterparts in both accuracy and efficiency.
summary: Graph contrastive learning (GCL) alleviates the heavy reliance on label information for graph representation learning (GRL) via self-supervised learning schemes. The core idea is to learn by maximising mutual information for similar instances, which requires similarity computation between two node instances. However, GCL is inefficient in both time and memory consumption. In addition, GCL normally requires a large number of training epochs to be well-trained on large-scale datasets. Inspired by an observation of a technical defect (i.e., inappropriate usage of Sigmoid function) commonly used in two representative GCL works, DGI and MVGRL, we revisit GCL and introduce a new learning paradigm for self-supervised graph representation learning, namely, Group Discrimination (GD), and propose a novel GD-based method called Graph Group Discrimination (GGD). Instead of similarity computation, GGD directly discriminates two groups of node samples with a very simple binary cross-entropy loss. In addition, GGD requires much fewer training epochs to obtain competitive performance compared with GCL methods on large-scale datasets. These two advantages endow GGD with very efficient property. Extensive experiments show that GGD outperforms state-of-the-art self-supervised methods on eight datasets. In particular, GGD can be trained in 0.18 seconds (6.44 seconds including data preprocessing) on ogbn-arxiv, which is orders of magnitude (10,000+) faster than GCL baselines while consuming much less memory. Trained with 9 hours on ogbn-papers100M with billion edges, GGD outperforms its GCL counterparts in both accuracy and efficiency.
abstract_zh: 图对比学习（GCL）通过自监督学习方案减轻了图表示学习（GRL）对标签信息的严重依赖。其核心思想是通过最大化相似实例的互信息来学习，这需要两个节点实例之间的相似度计算。然而，GCL 在时间和内存消耗方面效率都很低。此外，GCL 通常需要大量的训练 epoch 才能在大规模数据集上得到良好的训练。受到对两个代表性 GCL 作品 DGI 和 MVGRL 中常用的技术缺陷（即 Sigmoid 函数的不当使用）的观察的启发，我们重新审视 GCL，引入一种新的自监督图表示学习的学习范式，即组判别（GD），并提出一种基于 GD 的新颖方法，称为图组判别（GGD）。 GGD 不使用相似度计算，而是通过非常简单的二元交叉熵损失直接区分两组节点样本。此外，与大规模数据集上的 GCL 方法相比，GGD 需要更少的训练周期来获得有竞争力的性能。这两个优点赋予了GGD非常高效的性能。大量实验表明，GGD 在八个数据集上的表现优于最先进的自监督方法。特别是，GGD 在 ogbn-arxiv 上的训练时间为 0.18 秒（包括数据预处理为 6.44 秒），比 GCL 基线快几个数量级（10,000+），同时消耗的内存少得多。 GGD 在具有 10 亿条边的 ogbn-papers100M 上进行了 9 个小时的训练，在准确性和效率上都优于 GCL 同行。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种高效的图对比学习方法Graph Group Discrimination (GGD)，通过组别歧视策略解决现有方法在时间、内存和训练轮次上的低效问题。
AI.motivation: 现有图对比学习(GCL)方法依赖相似性计算，导致时间/内存消耗大且需要大量训练轮次，且发现DGI/MVGRL中Sigmoid函数使用存在技术缺陷。
AI.method: 提出组别歧视(GD)新范式，用二元交叉熵损失直接区分两组节点样本，替代传统相似性计算，显著降低计算复杂度。
AI.result: 在8个数据集上优于现有方法，ogbn-arxiv训练速度达10,000+倍提升(0.18秒/次)，ogbn-papers100M数据集上同时实现更高精度与效率。
AI.conclusion: GGD通过简单损失函数设计和训练策略优化，在保持性能的同时实现图表示学习的高效规模化，为大规模图数据处理提供新思路。

authors_text: Mutian He,Tianqing Fang,Weiqi Wang,Yangqiu Song
title: Acquiring and Modelling Abstract Commonsense Knowledge via Conceptualization
title_zh: 通过概念化获取和建模抽象常识知识
comment: 43 pages, 11 figures; Accepted by Artificial Intelligence
comments: 43 pages, 11 figures; Accepted by Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Conceptualization, or viewing entities and situations as instances of abstract concepts in mind and making inferences based on that, is a vital component in human intelligence for commonsense reasoning. Despite recent progress in artificial intelligence to acquire and model commonsense attributed to neural language models and commonsense knowledge graphs (CKGs), conceptualization is yet to be introduced thoroughly, making current approaches ineffective to cover knowledge about countless diverse entities and situations in the real world. To address the problem, we thoroughly study the role of conceptualization in commonsense reasoning, and formulate a framework to replicate human conceptual induction by acquiring abstract knowledge about events regarding abstract concepts, as well as higher-level triples or inferences upon them. We then apply the framework to ATOMIC, a large-scale human-annotated CKG, aided by the taxonomy Probase. We annotate a dataset on the validity of contextualized conceptualizations from ATOMIC on both event and triple levels, develop a series of heuristic rules based on linguistic features, and train a set of neural models to generate and verify abstract knowledge. Based on these components, a pipeline to acquire abstract knowledge is built. A large abstract CKG upon ATOMIC is then induced, ready to be instantiated to infer about unseen entities or situations. Finally, we empirically show the benefits of augmenting CKGs with abstract knowledge in downstream tasks like commonsense inference and zero-shot commonsense QA.
summary: Conceptualization, or viewing entities and situations as instances of abstract concepts in mind and making inferences based on that, is a vital component in human intelligence for commonsense reasoning. Despite recent progress in artificial intelligence to acquire and model commonsense attributed to neural language models and commonsense knowledge graphs (CKGs), conceptualization is yet to be introduced thoroughly, making current approaches ineffective to cover knowledge about countless diverse entities and situations in the real world. To address the problem, we thoroughly study the role of conceptualization in commonsense reasoning, and formulate a framework to replicate human conceptual induction by acquiring abstract knowledge about events regarding abstract concepts, as well as higher-level triples or inferences upon them. We then apply the framework to ATOMIC, a large-scale human-annotated CKG, aided by the taxonomy Probase. We annotate a dataset on the validity of contextualized conceptualizations from ATOMIC on both event and triple levels, develop a series of heuristic rules based on linguistic features, and train a set of neural models to generate and verify abstract knowledge. Based on these components, a pipeline to acquire abstract knowledge is built. A large abstract CKG upon ATOMIC is then induced, ready to be instantiated to infer about unseen entities or situations. Finally, we empirically show the benefits of augmenting CKGs with abstract knowledge in downstream tasks like commonsense inference and zero-shot commonsense QA.
abstract_zh: 概念化，或者将实体和情况视为头脑中抽象概念的实例并据此做出推论，是人类智力进行常识推理的重要组成部分。尽管人工智能最近在获取和建模归因于神经语言模型和常识知识图（CKG）的常识方面取得了进展，但概念化尚未彻底引入，使得当前的方法无法有效地涵盖现实世界中无数不同实体和情况的知识。为了解决这个问题，我们深入研究了概念化在常识推理中的作用，并制定了一个框架，通过获取有关抽象概念的事件的抽象知识以及更高层次的三元组或对它们的推理来复制人类概念归纳。然后，我们在分类 Probase 的帮助下将该框架应用于 ATOMIC，这是一个大规模的人工注释 CKG。我们在事件和三元级别上注释了 ATOMIC 情境化概念化有效性的数据集，开发了一系列基于语言特征的启发式规则，并训练了一组神经模型来生成和验证抽象知识。基于这些组件，构建了获取抽象知识的管道。然后诱导 ATOMIC 上的大型抽象 CKG，准备实例化以推断未见过的实体或情况。最后，我们凭经验展示了在常识推理和零样本常识 QA 等下游任务中用抽象知识增强 CKG 的好处。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过概念化方法获取和建模抽象常识知识，以增强常识推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有神经语言模型和常识知识图谱（CKGs）难以覆盖现实世界中大量多样化实体和情境的常识知识，需引入概念化机制以提升推理效果。
AI.method: 构建概念化框架，结合ATOMIC知识库与Probase分类体系，通过标注数据集、设计语言特征启发式规则及训练神经模型，生成并验证抽象常识知识。
AI.result: 生成大规模抽象常识知识图谱，并在常识推理和零样本问答等下游任务中验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 将抽象知识融入常识知识图谱能显著提升模型对未见实体或情境的推理能力。

search_text: Acquiring and Modelling Abstract Commonsense Knowledge via Conceptualization 通过概念化获取和建模抽象常识知识 Conceptualization, or viewing entities and situations as instances of abstract concepts in mind and making inferences based on that, is a vital component in human intelligence for commonsense reasoning. Despite recent progress in artificial intelligence to acquire and model commonsense attributed to neural language models and commonsense knowledge graphs (CKGs), conceptualization is yet to be introduced thoroughly, making current approaches ineffective to cover knowledge about countless diverse entities and situations in the real world. To address the problem, we thoroughly study the role of conceptualization in commonsense reasoning, and formulate a framework to replicate human conceptual induction by acquiring abstract knowledge about events regarding abstract concepts, as well as higher-level triples or inferences upon them. We then apply the framework to ATOMIC, a large-scale human-annotated CKG, aided by the taxonomy Probase. We annotate a dataset on the validity of contextualized conceptualizations from ATOMIC on both event and triple levels, develop a series of heuristic rules based on linguistic features, and train a set of neural models to generate and verify abstract knowledge. Based on these components, a pipeline to acquire abstract knowledge is built. A large abstract CKG upon ATOMIC is then induced, ready to be instantiated to infer about unseen entities or situations. Finally, we empirically show the benefits of augmenting CKGs with abstract knowledge in downstream tasks like commonsense inference and zero-shot commonsense QA. 概念化，或者将实体和情况视为头脑中抽象概念的实例并据此做出推论，是人类智力进行常识推理的重要组成部分。尽管人工智能最近在获取和建模归因于神经语言模型和常识知识图（CKG）的常识方面取得了进展，但概念化尚未彻底引入，使得当前的方法无法有效地涵盖现实世界中无数不同实体和情况的知识。为了解决这个问题，我们深入研究了概念化在常识推理中的作用，并制定了一个框架，通过获取有关抽象概念的事件的抽象知识以及更高层次的三元组或对它们的推理来复制人类概念归纳。然后，我们在分类 Probase 的帮助下将该框架应用于 ATOMIC，这是一个大规模的人工注释 CKG。我们在事件和三元级别上注释了 ATOMIC 情境化概念化有效性的数据集，开发了一系列基于语言特征的启发式规则，并训练了一组神经模型来生成和验证抽象知识。基于这些组件，构建了获取抽象知识的管道。然后诱导 ATOMIC 上的大型抽象 CKG，准备实例化以推断未见过的实体或情况。最后，我们凭经验展示了在常识推理和零样本常识 QA 等下游任务中用抽象知识增强 CKG 的好处。 本文提出通过概念化方法获取和建模抽象常识知识，以增强常识推理能力。 Mutian He,Tianqing Fang,Weiqi Wang,Yangqiu Song cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Kapil Deshpande,Narinder Singh Punn,Sanjay Kumar Sonbhadra,Sonali Agarwal
title: Anomaly detection in surveillance videos using transformer based attention model
title_zh: 使用基于变压器的注意力模型进行监控视频中的异常检测
abstract: Surveillance footage can catch a wide range of realistic anomalies. This research suggests using a weakly supervised strategy to avoid annotating anomalous segments in training videos, which is time consuming. In this approach only video level labels are used to obtain frame level anomaly scores. Weakly supervised video anomaly detection (WSVAD) suffers from the wrong identification of abnormal and normal instances during the training process. Therefore it is important to extract better quality features from the available videos. WIth this motivation, the present paper uses better quality transformer-based features named Videoswin Features followed by the attention layer based on dilated convolution and self attention to capture long and short range dependencies in temporal domain. This gives us a better understanding of available videos. The proposed framework is validated on real-world dataset i.e. ShanghaiTech Campus dataset which results in competitive performance than current state-of-the-art methods. The model and the code are available at https://github.com/kapildeshpande/Anomaly-Detection-in-Surveillance-Videos
summary: Surveillance footage can catch a wide range of realistic anomalies. This research suggests using a weakly supervised strategy to avoid annotating anomalous segments in training videos, which is time consuming. In this approach only video level labels are used to obtain frame level anomaly scores. Weakly supervised video anomaly detection (WSVAD) suffers from the wrong identification of abnormal and normal instances during the training process. Therefore it is important to extract better quality features from the available videos. WIth this motivation, the present paper uses better quality transformer-based features named Videoswin Features followed by the attention layer based on dilated convolution and self attention to capture long and short range dependencies in temporal domain. This gives us a better understanding of available videos. The proposed framework is validated on real-world dataset i.e. ShanghaiTech Campus dataset which results in competitive performance than current state-of-the-art methods. The model and the code are available at https://github.com/kapildeshpande/Anomaly-Detection-in-Surveillance-Videos
abstract_zh: 监控录像可以捕捉到各种现实的异常现象。这项研究建议使用弱监督策略来避免在训练视频中注释异常片段，这非常耗时。在这种方法中，仅使用视频级标签来获取帧级异常分数。弱监督视频异常检测（WSVAD）在训练过程中会错误识别异常和正常实例。因此，从可用视频中提取质量更好的特征非常重要。出于这个动机，本文使用质量更好的基于转换器的特征，称为 Videoswin Features，然后是基于扩张卷积和自注意力的注意力层，以捕获时域中的长程和短程依赖性。这使我们可以更好地了解可用视频。所提出的框架在真实世界数据集（即上海科技大学校园数据集）上进行了验证，其性能比当前最先进的方法具有竞争力。该模型和代码可在 https://github.com/kapildeshpande/Anomaly-Detection-in-Surveillance-Videos 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer注意力模型的弱监督视频异常检测方法，通过视频级标签生成帧级异常分数，无需标注异常片段。
AI.motivation: 现有弱监督视频异常检测（WSVAD）方法在训练过程中易混淆正常与异常实例，需提取更高质量的视频特征以提升检测精度。
AI.method: 采用VideoSwin Transformer提取高质量特征，结合空洞卷积与自注意力机制捕捉时序长短期依赖关系，构建端到端检测框架。
AI.result: 在ShanghaiTech Campus真实数据集上达到优于当前最先进方法的性能，模型代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: Transformer特征与注意力机制有效提升了弱监督场景下的视频异常检测能力，为实际安防应用提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Shiqing Liu,Haoyu Zhang,Yaochu Jin
title: A Survey on Computationally Efficient Neural Architecture Search
title_zh: 计算高效的神经架构搜索调查
abstract: Neural architecture search (NAS) has become increasingly popular in the deep learning community recently, mainly because it can provide an opportunity to allow interested users without rich expertise to benefit from the success of deep neural networks (DNNs). However, NAS is still laborious and time-consuming because a large number of performance estimations are required during the search process of NAS, and training DNNs is computationally intensive. To solve this major limitation of NAS, improving the computational efficiency is essential in the design of NAS. However, a systematic overview of computationally efficient NAS (CE-NAS) methods still lacks. To fill this gap, we provide a comprehensive survey of the state-of-the-art on CE-NAS by categorizing the existing work into proxy-based and surrogate-assisted NAS methods, together with a thorough discussion of their design principles and a quantitative comparison of their performances and computational complexities. The remaining challenges and open research questions are also discussed, and promising research topics in this emerging field are suggested.
summary: Neural architecture search (NAS) has become increasingly popular in the deep learning community recently, mainly because it can provide an opportunity to allow interested users without rich expertise to benefit from the success of deep neural networks (DNNs). However, NAS is still laborious and time-consuming because a large number of performance estimations are required during the search process of NAS, and training DNNs is computationally intensive. To solve this major limitation of NAS, improving the computational efficiency is essential in the design of NAS. However, a systematic overview of computationally efficient NAS (CE-NAS) methods still lacks. To fill this gap, we provide a comprehensive survey of the state-of-the-art on CE-NAS by categorizing the existing work into proxy-based and surrogate-assisted NAS methods, together with a thorough discussion of their design principles and a quantitative comparison of their performances and computational complexities. The remaining challenges and open research questions are also discussed, and promising research topics in this emerging field are suggested.
abstract_zh: 神经架构搜索（NAS）最近在深度学习社区中变得越来越流行，主要是因为它可以提供一个机会，让没有丰富专业知识的感兴趣的用户从深度神经网络（DNN）的成功中受益。然而，NAS仍然费力且耗时，因为NAS的搜索过程需要大量的性能估计，并且训练DNN是计算密集型的。为了解决NAS的这一主要局限性，在NAS的设计中提高计算效率至关重要。然而，仍然缺乏对计算高效的 NAS (CE-NAS) 方法的系统概述。为了填补这一空白，我们通过将现有工作分类为基于代理和代理辅助的 NAS 方法，对 CE-NAS 的最新技术进行了全面的调查，并对它们的设计原理进行了彻底的讨论，并对它们的性能和计算复杂性进行了定量比较。还讨论了剩余的挑战和开放的研究问题，并提出了这个新兴领域有前景的研究主题。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了计算高效神经架构搜索（CE-NAS）的最新方法，旨在解决传统NAS计算成本高的问题。
AI.motivation: 神经架构搜索（NAS）虽能帮助非专家用户受益于深度学习，但其搜索过程需要大量计算资源，亟需提升计算效率。
AI.method: 通过将现有CE-NAS方法分为基于代理和基于替代模型的两类，系统分析其设计原理，并定量比较性能与复杂度。
AI.result: 提供了CE-NAS领域的全面综述，总结了主流方法的优缺点及计算效率对比，明确了当前研究的不足。
AI.conclusion: 未来需进一步优化代理模型设计、探索新型搜索策略，并解决跨任务泛化等开放性问题。

ai_content_json.result: 提供了CE-NAS领域的全面综述，总结了主流方法的优缺点及计算效率对比，明确了当前研究的不足。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 未来需进一步优化代理模型设计、探索新型搜索策略，并解决跨任务泛化等开放性问题。

tldr: 本文综述了计算高效神经架构搜索（CE-NAS）的最新方法，旨在解决传统NAS计算成本高的问题。
motivation: 神经架构搜索（NAS）虽能帮助非专家用户受益于深度学习，但其搜索过程需要大量计算资源，亟需提升计算效率。
method: 通过将现有CE-NAS方法分为基于代理和基于替代模型的两类，系统分析其设计原理，并定量比较性能与复杂度。
result: 提供了CE-NAS领域的全面综述，总结了主流方法的优缺点及计算效率对比，明确了当前研究的不足。
conclusion: 未来需进一步优化代理模型设计、探索新型搜索策略，并解决跨任务泛化等开放性问题。
search_text: A Survey on Computationally Efficient Neural Architecture Search 计算高效的神经架构搜索调查 Neural architecture search (NAS) has become increasingly popular in the deep learning community recently, mainly because it can provide an opportunity to allow interested users without rich expertise to benefit from the success of deep neural networks (DNNs). However, NAS is still laborious and time-consuming because a large number of performance estimations are required during the search process of NAS, and training DNNs is computationally intensive. To solve this major limitation of NAS, improving the computational efficiency is essential in the design of NAS. However, a systematic overview of computationally efficient NAS (CE-NAS) methods still lacks. To fill this gap, we provide a comprehensive survey of the state-of-the-art on CE-NAS by categorizing the existing work into proxy-based and surrogate-assisted NAS methods, together with a thorough discussion of their design principles and a quantitative comparison of their performances and computational complexities. The remaining challenges and open research questions are also discussed, and promising research topics in this emerging field are suggested. 神经架构搜索（NAS）最近在深度学习社区中变得越来越流行，主要是因为它可以提供一个机会，让没有丰富专业知识的感兴趣的用户从深度神经网络（DNN）的成功中受益。然而，NAS仍然费力且耗时，因为NAS的搜索过程需要大量的性能估计，并且训练DNN是计算密集型的。为了解决NAS的这一主要局限性，在NAS的设计中提高计算效率至关重要。然而，仍然缺乏对计算高效的 NAS (CE-NAS) 方法的系统概述。为了填补这一空白，我们通过将现有工作分类为基于代理和代理辅助的 NAS 方法，对 CE-NAS 的最新技术进行了全面的调查，并对它们的设计原理进行了彻底的讨论，并对它们的性能和计算复杂性进行了定量比较。还讨论了剩余的挑战和开放的研究问题，并提出了这个新兴领域有前景的研究主题。 本文综述了计算高效神经架构搜索（CE-NAS）的最新方法，旨在解决传统NAS计算成本高的问题。 Shiqing Liu,Haoyu Zhang,Yaochu Jin cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: Yeseung Kim,Jinwoo Kim,Daehyung Park
title: GraphDistNet: A Graph-based Collision-distance Estimator for Gradient-based Trajectory Optimization
title_zh: GraphDistNet：基于图的碰撞距离估计器，用于基于梯度的轨迹优化
abstract: Trajectory optimization (TO) aims to find a sequence of valid states while minimizing costs. However, its fine validation process is often costly due to computationally expensive collision searches, otherwise coarse searches lower the safety of the system losing a precise solution. To resolve the issues, we introduce a new collision-distance estimator, GraphDistNet, that can precisely encode the structural information between two geometries by leveraging edge feature-based convolutional operations, and also efficiently predict a batch of collision distances and gradients through 25,000 random environments with a maximum of 20 unforeseen objects. Further, we show the adoption of attention mechanism enables our method to be easily generalized in unforeseen complex geometries toward TO. Our evaluation show GraphDistNet outperforms state-of-the-art baseline methods in both simulated and real world tasks.
summary: Trajectory optimization (TO) aims to find a sequence of valid states while minimizing costs. However, its fine validation process is often costly due to computationally expensive collision searches, otherwise coarse searches lower the safety of the system losing a precise solution. To resolve the issues, we introduce a new collision-distance estimator, GraphDistNet, that can precisely encode the structural information between two geometries by leveraging edge feature-based convolutional operations, and also efficiently predict a batch of collision distances and gradients through 25,000 random environments with a maximum of 20 unforeseen objects. Further, we show the adoption of attention mechanism enables our method to be easily generalized in unforeseen complex geometries toward TO. Our evaluation show GraphDistNet outperforms state-of-the-art baseline methods in both simulated and real world tasks.
abstract_zh: 轨迹优化（TO）旨在找到一系列有效状态，同时最小化成本。然而，由于计算上昂贵的碰撞搜索，其精细验证过程通常成本高昂，否则粗略搜索会降低系统失去精确解决方案的安全性。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了一种新的碰撞距离估计器 GraphDistNet，它可以通过利用基于边缘特征的卷积运算来精确编码两个几何图形之间的结构信息，并且还可以通过 25,000 个随机环境（最多 20 个不可预见的对象）有效地预测一批碰撞距离和梯度。此外，我们展示了注意力机制的采用使我们的方法能够轻松地推广到 TO 的不可预见的复杂几何结构。我们的评估表明 GraphDistNet 在模拟和现实世界任务中都优于最先进的基线方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于图的碰撞距离估计器GraphDistNet，用于梯度轨迹优化，解决验证成本高与安全性不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 轨迹优化的验证过程因计算密集的碰撞检测导致成本高昂，而粗略检测又会降低系统安全性，需更高效精确的碰撞距离估计方法。
AI.method: 通过边特征卷积操作编码几何结构信息，结合注意力机制，在25000个随机环境中批量预测碰撞距离与梯度，提升复杂场景泛化能力。
AI.result: 在模拟与真实任务中，GraphDistNet优于现有方法，验证了其在效率与安全性上的优势。
AI.conclusion: GraphDistNet有效平衡了轨迹优化中的计算成本与安全需求，为复杂环境下的应用提供了可靠解决方案。

search_text: GraphDistNet: A Graph-based Collision-distance Estimator for Gradient-based Trajectory Optimization GraphDistNet：基于图的碰撞距离估计器，用于基于梯度的轨迹优化 Trajectory optimization (TO) aims to find a sequence of valid states while minimizing costs. However, its fine validation process is often costly due to computationally expensive collision searches, otherwise coarse searches lower the safety of the system losing a precise solution. To resolve the issues, we introduce a new collision-distance estimator, GraphDistNet, that can precisely encode the structural information between two geometries by leveraging edge feature-based convolutional operations, and also efficiently predict a batch of collision distances and gradients through 25,000 random environments with a maximum of 20 unforeseen objects. Further, we show the adoption of attention mechanism enables our method to be easily generalized in unforeseen complex geometries toward TO. Our evaluation show GraphDistNet outperforms state-of-the-art baseline methods in both simulated and real world tasks. 轨迹优化（TO）旨在找到一系列有效状态，同时最小化成本。然而，由于计算上昂贵的碰撞搜索，其精细验证过程通常成本高昂，否则粗略搜索会降低系统失去精确解决方案的安全性。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了一种新的碰撞距离估计器 GraphDistNet，它可以通过利用基于边缘特征的卷积运算来精确编码两个几何图形之间的结构信息，并且还可以通过 25,000 个随机环境（最多 20 个不可预见的对象）有效地预测一批碰撞距离和梯度。此外，我们展示了注意力机制的采用使我们的方法能够轻松地推广到 TO 的不可预见的复杂几何结构。我们的评估表明 GraphDistNet 在模拟和现实世界任务中都优于最先进的基线方法。 本文提出一种基于图的碰撞距离估计器GraphDistNet，用于梯度轨迹优化，解决验证成本高与安全性不足的问题。 Yeseung Kim,Jinwoo Kim,Daehyung Park cs.RO

authors_text: Shiye Lei,Fengxiang He,Yancheng Yuan,Dacheng Tao
title: Understanding Deep Learning via Decision Boundary
title_zh: 通过决策边界理解深度学习
abstract: This paper discovers that the neural network with lower decision boundary (DB) variability has better generalizability. Two new notions, algorithm DB variability and $(ε, η)$-data DB variability, are proposed to measure the decision boundary variability from the algorithm and data perspectives. Extensive experiments show significant negative correlations between the decision boundary variability and the generalizability. From the theoretical view, two lower bounds based on algorithm DB variability are proposed and do not explicitly depend on the sample size. We also prove an upper bound of order $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{m}}+ε+η\log\frac{1}η\right)$ based on data DB variability. The bound is convenient to estimate without the requirement of labels, and does not explicitly depend on the network size which is usually prohibitively large in deep learning.
summary: This paper discovers that the neural network with lower decision boundary (DB) variability has better generalizability. Two new notions, algorithm DB variability and $(ε, η)$-data DB variability, are proposed to measure the decision boundary variability from the algorithm and data perspectives. Extensive experiments show significant negative correlations between the decision boundary variability and the generalizability. From the theoretical view, two lower bounds based on algorithm DB variability are proposed and do not explicitly depend on the sample size. We also prove an upper bound of order $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{m}}+ε+η\log\frac{1}η\right)$ based on data DB variability. The bound is convenient to estimate without the requirement of labels, and does not explicitly depend on the network size which is usually prohibitively large in deep learning.
abstract_zh: 本文发现决策边界（DB）变异性较低的神经网络具有更好的泛化性。提出了两个新概念：算法数据库变异性和$(ε, η)$-数据数据库变异性，以从算法和数据角度衡量决策边界变异性。大量实验表明决策边界变异性和概括性之间存在显着的负相关性。从理论角度来看，提出了两个基于算法数据库变异性的下限，并且不明确取决于样本大小。我们还基于数据数据库的可变性证明了 $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{m}}+ε+η\log\frac{1}η\right)$ 阶的上限。该界限无需标签即可方便地估计，并且不明确依赖于深度学习中通常过大的网络大小。

AI.tldr: 本文通过研究决策边界（DB）的可变性，探讨其与深度学习模型泛化能力的关系，并提出新的理论分析框架。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对神经网络决策边界稳定性的系统分析，而泛化能力与决策边界特性可能存在密切关联，需从算法和数据角度量化该特性。
AI.method: 提出算法DB可变性和(ε,η)-数据DB可变性两个新指标，结合实验验证与理论分析，建立与泛化能力的联系并推导相关界。
AI.result: 实验显示DB可变性与泛化能力呈显著负相关；理论推导出与样本量无关的下界及无需标签的上界估计。
AI.conclusion: 决策边界稳定性是影响泛化能力的关键因素，所提理论界为模型分析提供了更高效且实用的评估工具。

authors_text: Yao Fu,Mirella Lapata
title: Latent Topology Induction for Understanding Contextualized Representations
title_zh: 用于理解上下文表示的潜在拓扑归纳
abstract: In this work, we study the representation space of contextualized embeddings and gain insight into the hidden topology of large language models. We show there exists a network of latent states that summarize linguistic properties of contextualized representations. Instead of seeking alignments to existing well-defined annotations, we infer this latent network in a fully unsupervised way using a structured variational autoencoder. The induced states not only serve as anchors that mark the topology (neighbors and connectivity) of the representation manifold but also reveal the internal mechanism of encoding sentences. With the induced network, we: (1). decompose the representation space into a spectrum of latent states which encode fine-grained word meanings with lexical, morphological, syntactic and semantic information; (2). show state-state transitions encode rich phrase constructions and serve as the backbones of the latent space. Putting the two together, we show that sentences are represented as a traversal over the latent network where state-state transition chains encode syntactic templates and state-word emissions fill in the content. We demonstrate these insights with extensive experiments and visualizations.
summary: In this work, we study the representation space of contextualized embeddings and gain insight into the hidden topology of large language models. We show there exists a network of latent states that summarize linguistic properties of contextualized representations. Instead of seeking alignments to existing well-defined annotations, we infer this latent network in a fully unsupervised way using a structured variational autoencoder. The induced states not only serve as anchors that mark the topology (neighbors and connectivity) of the representation manifold but also reveal the internal mechanism of encoding sentences. With the induced network, we: (1). decompose the representation space into a spectrum of latent states which encode fine-grained word meanings with lexical, morphological, syntactic and semantic information; (2). show state-state transitions encode rich phrase constructions and serve as the backbones of the latent space. Putting the two together, we show that sentences are represented as a traversal over the latent network where state-state transition chains encode syntactic templates and state-word emissions fill in the content. We demonstrate these insights with extensive experiments and visualizations.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们研究了上下文嵌入的表示空间，并深入了解大型语言模型的隐藏拓扑。我们证明存在一个潜在状态网络，它总结了上下文表征的语言特性。我们没有寻求与现有明确定义的注释的对齐，而是使用结构化变分自动编码器以完全无监督的方式推断这个潜在网络。诱导状态不仅充当标记表示流形的拓扑（邻居和连通性）的锚，而且揭示了编码句子的内部机制。通过诱导网络，我们：（1）。将表示空间分解为一系列潜在状态，这些潜在状态用词汇、形态、句法和语义信息编码细粒度的词义； （2）。显示状态-状态转换编码丰富的短语结构并充当潜在空间的支柱。将两者放在一起，我们表明句子被表示为对潜在网络的遍历，其中状态-状态转换链对句法模板进行编码，并且状态词发射填充内容。我们通过大量的实验和可视化展示了这些见解。

AI.tldr: 通过诱导潜在拓扑结构揭示上下文表示的隐藏网络，分解表示空间并解析句子编码机制。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖预定义注释无法全面捕捉语言模型内部结构，需探索无监督方式揭示隐藏拓扑。
AI.method: 采用结构化变分自编码器（VAE）在无监督条件下推断潜在状态网络，分解表示空间并构建状态转换模型。
AI.result: 分解表示空间为潜在状态谱，状态转换编码短语结构，句子表示为潜在网络的遍历，验证了拓扑结构对编码机制的解释力。
AI.conclusion: 提出无监督方法揭示语言模型潜在拓扑结构，为理解上下文表示提供新视角与解析框架。

search_text: Latent Topology Induction for Understanding Contextualized Representations 用于理解上下文表示的潜在拓扑归纳 In this work, we study the representation space of contextualized embeddings and gain insight into the hidden topology of large language models. We show there exists a network of latent states that summarize linguistic properties of contextualized representations. Instead of seeking alignments to existing well-defined annotations, we infer this latent network in a fully unsupervised way using a structured variational autoencoder. The induced states not only serve as anchors that mark the topology (neighbors and connectivity) of the representation manifold but also reveal the internal mechanism of encoding sentences. With the induced network, we: (1). decompose the representation space into a spectrum of latent states which encode fine-grained word meanings with lexical, morphological, syntactic and semantic information; (2). show state-state transitions encode rich phrase constructions and serve as the backbones of the latent space. Putting the two together, we show that sentences are represented as a traversal over the latent network where state-state transition chains encode syntactic templates and state-word emissions fill in the content. We demonstrate these insights with extensive experiments and visualizations. 在这项工作中，我们研究了上下文嵌入的表示空间，并深入了解大型语言模型的隐藏拓扑。我们证明存在一个潜在状态网络，它总结了上下文表征的语言特性。我们没有寻求与现有明确定义的注释的对齐，而是使用结构化变分自动编码器以完全无监督的方式推断这个潜在网络。诱导状态不仅充当标记表示流形的拓扑（邻居和连通性）的锚，而且揭示了编码句子的内部机制。通过诱导网络，我们：（1）。将表示空间分解为一系列潜在状态，这些潜在状态用词汇、形态、句法和语义信息编码细粒度的词义； （2）。显示状态-状态转换编码丰富的短语结构并充当潜在空间的支柱。将两者放在一起，我们表明句子被表示为对潜在网络的遍历，其中状态-状态转换链对句法模板进行编码，并且状态词发射填充内容。我们通过大量的实验和可视化展示了这些见解。 通过诱导潜在拓扑结构揭示上下文表示的隐藏网络，分解表示空间并解析句子编码机制。 Yao Fu,Mirella Lapata cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: Umm-e-Habiba,Justus Bogner,Stefan Wagner
title: Can Requirements Engineering Support Explainable Artificial Intelligence? Towards a User-Centric Approach for Explainability Requirements
title_zh: 需求工程可以支持可解释的人工智能吗？针对可解释性要求采用以用户为中心的方法
abstract: With the recent proliferation of artificial intelligence systems, there has been a surge in the demand for explainability of these systems. Explanations help to reduce system opacity, support transparency, and increase stakeholder trust. In this position paper, we discuss synergies between requirements engineering (RE) and Explainable AI (XAI). We highlight challenges in the field of XAI, and propose a framework and research directions on how RE practices can help to mitigate these challenges.
summary: With the recent proliferation of artificial intelligence systems, there has been a surge in the demand for explainability of these systems. Explanations help to reduce system opacity, support transparency, and increase stakeholder trust. In this position paper, we discuss synergies between requirements engineering (RE) and Explainable AI (XAI). We highlight challenges in the field of XAI, and propose a framework and research directions on how RE practices can help to mitigate these challenges.
abstract_zh: 随着最近人工智能系统的激增，对这些系统的可解释性的需求激增。解释有助于减少系统不透明度、支持透明度并增加利益相关者的信任。在这篇立场文件中，我们讨论了需求工程 (RE) 和可解释人工智能 (XAI) 之间的协同作用。我们强调 XAI 领域的挑战，并提出关于可再生能源实践如何帮助缓解这些挑战的框架和研究方向。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨需求工程（RE）如何支持可解释人工智能（XAI），并提出用户为中心的可解释性需求框架与研究方向。
AI.motivation: 人工智能系统普及引发对系统可解释性的迫切需求，而现有XAI研究在需求层面存在不足，需结合RE方法解决实际挑战。
AI.method: 通过立场论文形式分析XAI领域挑战，结合RE理论提出框架与未来研究方向。
AI.result: 构建了RE支持XAI的框架，明确了用户需求驱动的可解释性要求获取与验证方法。
AI.conclusion: 需求工程能有效支持XAI发展，需进一步探索用户视角下的可解释性需求建模与验证机制。

authors_text: Xiao Ruiqiang
title: YOLOv5s-GTB: light-weighted and improved YOLOv5s for bridge crack detection
title_zh: YOLOv5s-GTB：用于桥梁裂缝检测的轻量级和改进的 YOLOv5
abstract: In response to the situation that the conventional bridge crack manual detection method has a large amount of human and material resources wasted, this study is aimed to propose a light-weighted, high-precision, deep learning-based bridge apparent crack recognition model that can be deployed in mobile devices' scenarios. In order to enhance the performance of YOLOv5, firstly, the data augmentation methods are supplemented, and then the YOLOv5 series algorithm is trained to select a suitable basic framework. The YOLOv5s is identified as the basic framework for the light-weighted crack detection model through experiments for comparison and validation.By replacing the traditional DarkNet backbone network of YOLOv5s with GhostNet backbone network, introducing Transformer multi-headed self-attention mechanism and bi-directional feature pyramid network (BiFPN) to replace the commonly used feature pyramid network, the improved model not only has 42% fewer parameters and faster inference response, but also significantly outperforms the original model in terms of accuracy and mAP (8.5% and 1.1% improvement, respectively). Luckily each improved part has a positive impact on the result. This paper provides a feasible idea to establish a digital operation management system in the field of highway and bridge in the future and to implement the whole life cycle structure health monitoring of civil infrastructure in China.
summary: In response to the situation that the conventional bridge crack manual detection method has a large amount of human and material resources wasted, this study is aimed to propose a light-weighted, high-precision, deep learning-based bridge apparent crack recognition model that can be deployed in mobile devices' scenarios. In order to enhance the performance of YOLOv5, firstly, the data augmentation methods are supplemented, and then the YOLOv5 series algorithm is trained to select a suitable basic framework. The YOLOv5s is identified as the basic framework for the light-weighted crack detection model through experiments for comparison and validation.By replacing the traditional DarkNet backbone network of YOLOv5s with GhostNet backbone network, introducing Transformer multi-headed self-attention mechanism and bi-directional feature pyramid network (BiFPN) to replace the commonly used feature pyramid network, the improved model not only has 42% fewer parameters and faster inference response, but also significantly outperforms the original model in terms of accuracy and mAP (8.5% and 1.1% improvement, respectively). Luckily each improved part has a positive impact on the result. This paper provides a feasible idea to establish a digital operation management system in the field of highway and bridge in the future and to implement the whole life cycle structure health monitoring of civil infrastructure in China.
abstract_zh: 针对传统桥梁裂缝人工检测方法浪费大量人力物力的情况，本研究旨在提出一种轻量级、高精度、基于深度学习的、可部署在移动设备场景的桥梁明显裂缝识别模型。为了增强YOLOv5的性能，首先补充数据增强方法，然后对YOLOv5系列算法进行训练，选择合适的基础框架。通过实验对比和验证，确定YOLOv5s为轻量级裂纹检测模型的基本框架。通过用GhostNet主干网络替代YOLOv5s传统的DarkNet主干网络，引入Transformer多头自注意力机制和双向特征金字塔网络（BiFPN）替代常用的特征金字塔网络，改进后的模型不仅参数减少了42%，推理响应更快，而且在准确率方面也明显优于原始模型和 mAP（分别提高 8.5% 和 1.1%）。幸运的是，每个改进的部分都会对结果产生积极的影响。为今后在公路桥梁领域建立数字化运营管理系统、实施我国民用基础设施全生命周期结构健康监测提供了可行的思路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量级改进的YOLOv5s模型（YOLOv5s-GTB），用于桥梁裂缝检测，适用于移动端部署。
AI.motivation: 传统桥梁裂缝人工检测方法耗费大量人力物力，亟需高效、精准的自动化检测方案。
AI.method: 通过数据增强优化YOLOv5框架，选用YOLOv5s作为基础模型，替换为GhostNet主干网络，引入Transformer多头自注意力机制及BiFPN特征金字塔网络。
AI.result: 模型参数减少42%，推理速度提升，精度（mAP）提高1.1%，准确率提升8.5%，各改进模块均对结果有正向贡献。
AI.conclusion: 为未来构建公路桥梁数字化运维系统及中国基础设施全生命周期健康监测提供可行方案。

Luckily each improved part has a positive impact on the result. This paper provides a feasible idea to establish a digital operation management system in the field of highway and bridge in the future and to implement the whole life cycle structure health monitoring of civil infrastructure in China. 针对传统桥梁裂缝人工检测方法浪费大量人力物力的情况，本研究旨在提出一种轻量级、高精度、基于深度学习的、可部署在移动设备场景的桥梁明显裂缝识别模型。为了增强YOLOv5的性能，首先补充数据增强方法，然后对YOLOv5系列算法进行训练，选择合适的基础框架。通过实验对比和验证，确定YOLOv5s为轻量级裂纹检测模型的基本框架。通过用GhostNet主干网络替代YOLOv5s传统的DarkNet主干网络，引入Transformer多头自注意力机制和双向特征金字塔网络（BiFPN）替代常用的特征金字塔网络，改进后的模型不仅参数减少了42%，推理响应更快，而且在准确率方面也明显优于原始模型和 mAP（分别提高 8.5% 和 1.1%）。幸运的是，每个改进的部分都会对结果产生积极的影响。为今后在公路桥梁领域建立数字化运营管理系统、实施我国民用基础设施全生命周期结构健康监测提供了可行的思路。 本文提出一种轻量级改进的YOLOv5s模型（YOLOv5s-GTB），用于桥梁裂缝检测，适用于移动端部署。 Xiao Ruiqiang cs.CV

authors_text: Hristo Petkov,Colin Hanley,Feng Dong
title: Causality Learning With Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks
title_zh: 使用 Wasserstein 生成对抗网络进行因果关系学习
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2204.00387
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2204.00387
abstract: Conventional methods for causal structure learning from data face significant challenges due to combinatorial search space. Recently, the problem has been formulated into a continuous optimization framework with an acyclicity constraint to learn Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs). Such a framework allows the utilization of deep generative models for causal structure learning to better capture the relations between data sample distributions and DAGs. However, so far no study has experimented with the use of Wasserstein distance in the context of causal structure learning. Our model named DAG-WGAN combines the Wasserstein-based adversarial loss with an acyclicity constraint in an auto-encoder architecture. It simultaneously learns causal structures while improving its data generation capability. We compare the performance of DAG-WGAN with other models that do not involve the Wasserstein metric in order to identify its contribution to causal structure learning. Our model performs better with high cardinality data according to our experiments.
summary: Conventional methods for causal structure learning from data face significant challenges due to combinatorial search space. Recently, the problem has been formulated into a continuous optimization framework with an acyclicity constraint to learn Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs). Such a framework allows the utilization of deep generative models for causal structure learning to better capture the relations between data sample distributions and DAGs. However, so far no study has experimented with the use of Wasserstein distance in the context of causal structure learning. Our model named DAG-WGAN combines the Wasserstein-based adversarial loss with an acyclicity constraint in an auto-encoder architecture. It simultaneously learns causal structures while improving its data generation capability. We compare the performance of DAG-WGAN with other models that do not involve the Wasserstein metric in order to identify its contribution to causal structure learning. Our model performs better with high cardinality data according to our experiments.
abstract_zh: 由于组合搜索空间，从数据中学习因果结构的传统方法面临着重大挑战。最近，该问题已被表述为具有非循环约束的连续优化框架，以学习有向无环图（DAG）。这样的框架允许利用深度生成模型进行因果结构学习，以更好地捕获数据样本分布和 DAG 之间的关系。然而，到目前为止，还没有研究在因果结构学习的背景下尝试使用 Wasserstein 距离。我们名为 DAG-WGAN 的模型将基于 Wasserstein 的对抗性损失与自动编码器架构中的非循环约束相结合。它同时学习因果结构，同时提高其数据生成能力。我们将 DAG-WGAN 与其他不涉及 Wasserstein 度量的模型的性能进行比较，以确定其对因果结构学习的贡献。根据我们的实验，我们的模型在高基数数据下表现更好。

AI.tldr: 提出一种结合Wasserstein生成对抗网络（WGAN）与无环约束的模型DAG-WGAN，用于改进高基数数据下的因果结构学习。
AI.motivation: 传统因果结构学习方法受限于组合搜索空间，现有连续优化框架未尝试Wasserstein距离，需探索其在因果学习中的潜力。
AI.method: 设计DAG-WGAN模型，将Wasserstein对抗损失与无环约束结合于自编码器架构，同步学习因果结构与数据生成能力。
AI.result: 实验表明DAG-WGAN在高基数数据集上优于非Wasserstein方法，验证了模型有效性。
AI.conclusion: Wasserstein距离的引入提升了因果结构学习性能，尤其适用于高基数数据场景，为未来研究提供新方向。

authors_text: Matthew R Jones,Timothy J Rogers,Elizabeth J Cross
title: Constraining Gaussian processes for physics-informed acoustic emission mapping
title_zh: 物理信息声发射映射的约束高斯过程
abstract: The automated localisation of damage in structures is a challenging but critical ingredient in the path towards predictive or condition-based maintenance of high value structures. The use of acoustic emission time of arrival mapping is a promising approach to this challenge, but is severely hindered by the need to collect a dense set of artificial acoustic emission measurements across the structure, resulting in a lengthy and often impractical data acquisition process. In this paper, we consider the use of physics-informed Gaussian processes for learning these maps to alleviate this problem. In the approach, the Gaussian process is constrained to the physical domain such that information relating to the geometry and boundary conditions of the structure are embedded directly into the learning process, returning a model that guarantees that any predictions made satisfy physically-consistent behaviour at the boundary. A number of scenarios that arise when training measurement acquisition is limited, including where training data are sparse, and also of limited coverage over the structure of interest. Using a complex plate-like structure as an experimental case study, we show that our approach significantly reduces the burden of data collection, where it is seen that incorporation of boundary condition knowledge significantly improves predictive accuracy as training observations are reduced, particularly when training measurements are not available across all parts of the structure.
summary: The automated localisation of damage in structures is a challenging but critical ingredient in the path towards predictive or condition-based maintenance of high value structures. The use of acoustic emission time of arrival mapping is a promising approach to this challenge, but is severely hindered by the need to collect a dense set of artificial acoustic emission measurements across the structure, resulting in a lengthy and often impractical data acquisition process. In this paper, we consider the use of physics-informed Gaussian processes for learning these maps to alleviate this problem. In the approach, the Gaussian process is constrained to the physical domain such that information relating to the geometry and boundary conditions of the structure are embedded directly into the learning process, returning a model that guarantees that any predictions made satisfy physically-consistent behaviour at the boundary. A number of scenarios that arise when training measurement acquisition is limited, including where training data are sparse, and also of limited coverage over the structure of interest. Using a complex plate-like structure as an experimental case study, we show that our approach significantly reduces the burden of data collection, where it is seen that incorporation of boundary condition knowledge significantly improves predictive accuracy as training observations are reduced, particularly when training measurements are not available across all parts of the structure.
abstract_zh: 结构损伤的自动定位是高价值结构的预测性或基于状态的维护过程中具有挑战性但又至关重要的因素。使用声发射到达时间测绘是应对这一挑战的一种有前景的方法，但由于需要在整个结构上收集一组密集的人工声发射测量值而受到严重阻碍，导致数据采集过程冗长且通常不切实际。在本文中，我们考虑使用基于物理的高斯过程来学习这些图来缓解这个问题。在该方法中，高斯过程被限制在物理域，使得与结构的几何形状和边界条件相关的信息直接嵌入到学习过程中，返回一个模型，保证所做的任何预测都满足边界处的物理一致行为。当训练测量获取有限时会出现许多场景，包括训练数据稀疏以及感兴趣结构的覆盖范围有限。使用复杂的板状结构作为实验案例研究，我们表明我们的方法显着减轻了数据收集的负担，其中可以看出，随着训练观测值的减少，边界条件知识的结合显着提高了预测准确性，特别是当结构的所有部分都无法获得训练测量值时。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物理信息的高斯过程方法，用于在数据有限的情况下进行声发射映射，以实现结构损伤的自动化定位。
AI.motivation: 传统声发射时间到达映射方法需要密集的人工测量数据，导致数据采集过程漫长且不切实际，亟需减少数据依赖性同时保持预测准确性。
AI.method: 通过将结构几何和边界条件直接嵌入高斯过程的物理约束中，构建符合物理规律的模型，即使在训练数据稀疏或覆盖不全时也能保证边界行为的一致性。
AI.result: 在复杂板状结构实验中，该方法显著降低了数据采集负担，当训练数据减少或分布不均时，结合边界条件的知识能显著提升预测精度。
AI.conclusion: 物理信息高斯过程有效结合了领域知识与数据学习，在数据受限场景下为结构健康监测提供了更高效且准确的解决方案。

authors_text: Na Li,Robert Ross
title: Transferring Studies Across Embodiments: A Case Study in Confusion Detection
title_zh: 跨实施例转移研究：混淆检测的案例研究
comment: 6 pages paper for the 1st Workshop on the representation, sharing and evaluation of multimodal agent interaction, link: https://cltl.github.io/mmai2022/
comments: 6 pages paper for the 1st Workshop on the representation, sharing and evaluation of multimodal agent interaction, link: https://cltl.github.io/mmai2022/
abstract: Human-robot studies are expensive to conduct and difficult to control, and as such researchers sometimes turn to human-avatar interaction in the hope of faster and cheaper data collection that can be transferred to the robot domain. In terms of our work, we are particularly interested in the challenge of detecting and modelling user confusion in interaction, and as part of this research programme, we conducted situated dialogue studies to investigate users' reactions in confusing scenarios that we give in both physical and virtual environments. In this paper, we present a combined review of these studies and the results that we observed across these two embodiments. For the physical embodiment, we used a Pepper Robot, while for the virtual modality, we used a 3D avatar. Our study shows that despite attitudinal differences and technical control limitations, there were a number of similarities detected in user behaviour and self-reporting results across embodiment options. This work suggests that, while avatar interaction is no true substitute for robot interaction studies, sufficient care in study design may allow well executed human-avatar studies to supplement more challenging human-robot studies.
summary: Human-robot studies are expensive to conduct and difficult to control, and as such researchers sometimes turn to human-avatar interaction in the hope of faster and cheaper data collection that can be transferred to the robot domain. In terms of our work, we are particularly interested in the challenge of detecting and modelling user confusion in interaction, and as part of this research programme, we conducted situated dialogue studies to investigate users' reactions in confusing scenarios that we give in both physical and virtual environments. In this paper, we present a combined review of these studies and the results that we observed across these two embodiments. For the physical embodiment, we used a Pepper Robot, while for the virtual modality, we used a 3D avatar. Our study shows that despite attitudinal differences and technical control limitations, there were a number of similarities detected in user behaviour and self-reporting results across embodiment options. This work suggests that, while avatar interaction is no true substitute for robot interaction studies, sufficient care in study design may allow well executed human-avatar studies to supplement more challenging human-robot studies.
abstract_zh: 人机研究的成本高昂且难以控制，因此研究人员有时会转向人机交互，希望能够更快、更便宜地收集数据，并将其转移到机器人领域。就我们的工作而言，我们对在交互中检测和建模用户困惑的挑战特别感兴趣，作为该研究计划的一部分，我们进行了情境对话研究，以调查用户在我们在物理和虚拟环境中给出的困惑场景中的反应。在本文中，我们对这些研究以及我们在这两个实施例中观察到的结果进行了综合回顾。对于物理实施例，我们使用了 Pepper 机器人，而对于虚拟形态，我们使用了 3D 化身。我们的研究表明，尽管存在态度差异和技术控制限制，但在不同实施例选项的用户行为和自我报告结果中发现了许多相似之处。这项工作表明，虽然化身交互并不能真正替代机器人交互研究，但研究设计中的充分关注可能允许良好执行的人类化身研究来补充更具挑战性的人类机器人研究。

AI.tldr: 本文通过对比物理机器人与虚拟角色在用户困惑检测中的表现，探讨跨实体研究的可行性。
AI.motivation: 传统人机交互研究成本高且难以控制，研究者希望通过虚拟角色交互降低成本并获取可迁移至机器人领域的数据。
AI.method: 在物理环境使用Pepper机器人，虚拟环境使用3D角色，开展情境对话实验，对比分析用户行为与自我报告结果。
AI.result: 尽管存在态度差异和技术限制，两种实体下用户行为和反馈结果呈现显著相似性。
AI.conclusion: 虚拟角色研究可作为机器人研究的补充，但需谨慎设计以确保结果有效性。

search_text: Transferring Studies Across Embodiments: A Case Study in Confusion Detection 跨实施例转移研究：混淆检测的案例研究 Human-robot studies are expensive to conduct and difficult to control, and as such researchers sometimes turn to human-avatar interaction in the hope of faster and cheaper data collection that can be transferred to the robot domain. In terms of our work, we are particularly interested in the challenge of detecting and modelling user confusion in interaction, and as part of this research programme, we conducted situated dialogue studies to investigate users' reactions in confusing scenarios that we give in both physical and virtual environments. In this paper, we present a combined review of these studies and the results that we observed across these two embodiments. For the physical embodiment, we used a Pepper Robot, while for the virtual modality, we used a 3D avatar. Our study shows that despite attitudinal differences and technical control limitations, there were a number of similarities detected in user behaviour and self-reporting results across embodiment options. This work suggests that, while avatar interaction is no true substitute for robot interaction studies, sufficient care in study design may allow well executed human-avatar studies to supplement more challenging human-robot studies. 人机研究的成本高昂且难以控制，因此研究人员有时会转向人机交互，希望能够更快、更便宜地收集数据，并将其转移到机器人领域。就我们的工作而言，我们对在交互中检测和建模用户困惑的挑战特别感兴趣，作为该研究计划的一部分，我们进行了情境对话研究，以调查用户在我们在物理和虚拟环境中给出的困惑场景中的反应。在本文中，我们对这些研究以及我们在这两个实施例中观察到的结果进行了综合回顾。对于物理实施例，我们使用了 Pepper 机器人，而对于虚拟形态，我们使用了 3D 化身。我们的研究表明，尽管存在态度差异和技术控制限制，但在不同实施例选项的用户行为和自我报告结果中发现了许多相似之处。这项工作表明，虽然化身交互并不能真正替代机器人交互研究，但研究设计中的充分关注可能允许良好执行的人类化身研究来补充更具挑战性的人类机器人研究。 本文通过对比物理机器人与虚拟角色在用户困惑检测中的表现，探讨跨实体研究的可行性。 Na Li,Robert Ross cs.HC,cs.AI,cs.CY,cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Se-Wook Yoo,Chan Kim,Jin-Woo Choi,Seong-Woo Kim,Seung-Woo Seo
title: GIN: Graph-based Interaction-aware Constraint Policy Optimization for Autonomous Driving
title_zh: GIN：基于图的自动驾驶交互感知约束策略优化
comment: 8 pages, 7 figures, 4 tables, submission to RA-L in 2022
comments: 8 pages, 7 figures, 4 tables, submission to RA-L in 2022
abstract: Applying reinforcement learning to autonomous driving entails particular challenges, primarily due to dynamically changing traffic flows. To address such challenges, it is necessary to quickly determine response strategies to the changing intentions of surrounding vehicles. This paper proposes a new policy optimization method for safe driving using graph-based interaction-aware constraints. In this framework, the motion prediction and control modules are trained simultaneously while sharing a latent representation that contains a social context. To reflect social interactions, we illustrate the movements of agents in graph form and filter the features with the graph convolution networks. This helps preserve the spatiotemporal locality of adjacent nodes. Furthermore, we create feedback loops to combine these two modules effectively. As a result, this approach encourages the learned controller to be safe from dynamic risks and renders the motion prediction robust to abnormal movements. In the experiment, we set up a navigation scenario comprising various situations with CARLA, an urban driving simulator. The experiments show state-of-the-art performance on navigation strategy and motion prediction compared to the baselines.
summary: Applying reinforcement learning to autonomous driving entails particular challenges, primarily due to dynamically changing traffic flows. To address such challenges, it is necessary to quickly determine response strategies to the changing intentions of surrounding vehicles. This paper proposes a new policy optimization method for safe driving using graph-based interaction-aware constraints. In this framework, the motion prediction and control modules are trained simultaneously while sharing a latent representation that contains a social context. To reflect social interactions, we illustrate the movements of agents in graph form and filter the features with the graph convolution networks. This helps preserve the spatiotemporal locality of adjacent nodes. Furthermore, we create feedback loops to combine these two modules effectively. As a result, this approach encourages the learned controller to be safe from dynamic risks and renders the motion prediction robust to abnormal movements. In the experiment, we set up a navigation scenario comprising various situations with CARLA, an urban driving simulator. The experiments show state-of-the-art performance on navigation strategy and motion prediction compared to the baselines.
abstract_zh: 将强化学习应用于自动驾驶会带来特殊的挑战，这主要是由于交通流的动态变化。为了应对这些挑战，有必要快速确定对周围车辆不断变化的意图的响应策略。本文提出了一种使用基于图的交互感知约束的安全驾驶新策略优化方法。在此框架中，运动预测和控制模块同时进行训练，同时共享包含社交上下文的潜在表示。为了反映社交互动，我们以图的形式说明代理的运动，并使用图卷积网络过滤特征。这有助于保留相邻节点的时空局部性。此外，我们创建反馈循环来有效地结合这两个模块。因此，这种方法鼓励学习控制器免受动态风险的影响，并使运动预测对异常运动具有鲁棒性。在实验中，我们使用城市驾驶模拟器CARLA设置了包含各种情况的导航场景。与基线相比，实验显示了导航策略和运动预测方面最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于图的交互感知约束策略优化方法，以提升自动驾驶在动态交通环境中的安全性和运动预测准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习在处理动态交通流和周围车辆意图变化时存在响应策略更新滞后的问题，需提升安全性和预测鲁棒性。
AI.method: 通过图卷积网络建模交通参与者交互关系，共享潜在表示实现运动预测与控制模块联合训练，并引入反馈循环机制强化策略约束。
AI.result: 在CARLA模拟器的多场景实验中，导航策略和运动预测性能达到SOTA水平，优于现有基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效结合了交互建模和约束优化，提升自动驾驶系统对动态风险的适应性与决策安全性。

authors_text: Thomas Gonzalez,Antoine Blais,Nicolas Couëllan,Christian Ruiz
title: Distributional loss for convolutional neural network regression and application to GNSS multi-path estimation
title_zh: 卷积神经网络回归的分布损失及其在 GNSS 多路径估计中的应用
abstract: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) have been widely used in image classification. Over the years, they have also benefited from various enhancements and they are now considered as state of the art techniques for image like data. However, when they are used for regression to estimate some function value from images, fewer recommendations are available. In this study, a novel CNN regression model is proposed. It combines convolutional neural layers to extract high level features representations from images with a soft labelling technique. More specifically, as the deep regression task is challenging, the idea is to account for some uncertainty in the targets that are seen as distributions around their mean. The estimations are carried out by the model in the form of distributions. Building from earlier work, a specific histogram loss function based on the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence is applied during training. The model takes advantage of the CNN feature representation and is able to carry out estimation from multi-channel input images. To assess and illustrate the technique, the model is applied to Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) multi-path estimation where multi-path signal parameters have to be estimated from correlator output images from the I and Q channels. The multi-path signal delay, magnitude, Doppler shift frequency and phase parameters are estimated from synthetically generated datasets of satellite signals. Experiments are conducted under various receiving conditions and various input images resolutions to test the estimation performances quality and robustness. The results show that the proposed soft labelling CNN technique using distributional loss outperforms classical CNN regression under all conditions. Furthermore, the extra learning performance achieved by the model allows the reduction of input image resolution from 80x80 down to 40x40 or sometimes 20x20.
summary: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) have been widely used in image classification. Over the years, they have also benefited from various enhancements and they are now considered as state of the art techniques for image like data. However, when they are used for regression to estimate some function value from images, fewer recommendations are available. In this study, a novel CNN regression model is proposed. It combines convolutional neural layers to extract high level features representations from images with a soft labelling technique. More specifically, as the deep regression task is challenging, the idea is to account for some uncertainty in the targets that are seen as distributions around their mean. The estimations are carried out by the model in the form of distributions. Building from earlier work, a specific histogram loss function based on the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence is applied during training. The model takes advantage of the CNN feature representation and is able to carry out estimation from multi-channel input images. To assess and illustrate the technique, the model is applied to Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) multi-path estimation where multi-path signal parameters have to be estimated from correlator output images from the I and Q channels. The multi-path signal delay, magnitude, Doppler shift frequency and phase parameters are estimated from synthetically generated datasets of satellite signals. Experiments are conducted under various receiving conditions and various input images resolutions to test the estimation performances quality and robustness. The results show that the proposed soft labelling CNN technique using distributional loss outperforms classical CNN regression under all conditions. Furthermore, the extra learning performance achieved by the model allows the reduction of input image resolution from 80x80 down to 40x40 or sometimes 20x20.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络（CNN）已广泛应用于图像分类。多年来，它们也受益于各种增强功能，现在被认为是图像类数据的最先进技术。然而，当它们用于回归来估计图像中的某些函数值时，可用的建议较少。在这项研究中，提出了一种新颖的 CNN 回归模型。它结合了卷积神经层，通过软标签技术从图像中提取高级特征表示。更具体地说，由于深度回归任务具有挑战性，因此我们的想法是考虑目标中的一些不确定性，这些不确定性被视为围绕其均值的分布。模型以分布的形式进行估计。在早期工作的基础上，在训练期间应用了基于 Kullback-Leibler (KL) 散度的特定直方图损失函数。该模型利用 CNN 特征表示，能够对多通道输入图像进行估计。为了评估和说明该技术，该模型应用于全球导航卫星系统 (GNSS) 多径估计，其中必须根据 I 和 Q 通道的相关器输出图像来估计多径信号参数。多径信号延迟、幅度、多普勒频移和相位参数是根据合成的卫星信号数据集估计的。在各种接收条件和各种输入图像分辨率下进行实验，以测试估计性能的质量和鲁棒性。结果表明，所提出的使用分布损失的软标记 CNN 技术在所有条件下都优于经典 CNN 回归。此外，该模型实现的额外学习性能允许将输入图像分辨率从 80x80 降低到 40x40 甚至有时 20x20。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于分布损失的卷积神经网络回归模型，并应用于GNSS多路径信号参数估计。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN在图像回归任务中缺乏有效指导，尤其难以处理目标值的不确定性问题。
AI.method: 结合卷积层特征提取与软标签技术，采用基于KL散度的直方图损失函数，将目标建模为分布进行回归估计。
AI.result: 在多种接收条件和输入分辨率下，该方法均优于传统CNN回归，且可将输入图像分辨率降至20x20。
AI.conclusion: 分布损失机制提升了CNN回归的鲁棒性，为GNSS多路径估计等高精度任务提供了新方法。
